revision.text:
{{日本の古墳 |名称= 七観山古墳 |画像= |所在地= 大阪府堺市堺区旭ヶ丘中町 |位置={{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|34|33|15|N|135|28|53|E|scale: }} |形状= 円墳 |規模=径56ｍ |築造年代= 5世紀 |埋葬施設=木棺 |被葬者= |出土品=鉄剣 甲冑 馬具 |史跡指定= |特記事項= }} '''七観山古墳'''（しちかんやまこふん）とは、[[大阪府]] [[堺市]] [[堺区]]旭ヶ丘中町4丁に所在した[[円墳]]で'''七観古墳'''ともいう。[[百舌鳥古墳群]]に属しており、[[大正時代]]と戦後に発掘され、大量の鉄製武器と甲冑が出土している。
== 概要== [[上石津ミサンザイ古墳]](伝[[履中天皇]]陵古墳)の後円部周濠の北側に位置し、同古墳の[[陪塚]]とされる七観音古墳のすぐ北西に所在していた。[[墳丘]]は２段築に築かれ、径56ｍ、高さ8ｍ、周濠も存在した。[[葺石]]と[[埴輪]]があり、墳頂部には鰭付[[円筒埴輪]]の方形埴輪列が巡っていたようである。また、家、短甲、蓋（きぬがさ） 靫(ゆき)などの形象埴輪も出土している。1913年（大正2年）、1947年（昭和22年）と発掘または遺物採取が行なわれて遺物が出土しているが、1952年（昭和27年）には土取り工事によって消滅することになり、それに先だって緊急の[[発掘調査]]が行なわれている。主体部は遺物をおさめた[[粘土槨]]のような施設が３ヶ所検出されている。出土した遺物は、鉄製甲冑（衝角付冑７、短甲6以上など）、鉄製素環頭大刀、鉄刀（130前後）、蛇行状鉄剣、鉄剣、鉄鋒、鉄鏃（百数十）などの大量の武器、武具が出土している。また鉄斧、手斧、やりがんな、金銅製帯金具、それに[[鐙などの[[馬具]]も出土している。そのほかに、滑石製勾玉、剣形鉄製品が採集されている。馬具の鐙は日本国内で出土している物の中でもっとも古いものとされ、また帯金具は龍文透彫鍔板（りゅうもんすかしぼりかばん）に心葉形垂飾（しんようけいすいしょく）の付いた優品としてよく知られている。この古墳の性格として、大量の副葬品の埋納にもかかわらず、この古墳には遺体が埋葬されなかった可能性があり、陪塚と考えられる古墳のあり方を示す貴重な一例となっている。なお、七観山古墳の跡地は現在、[[大仙公園]]の敷地にとり込まれ、消滅した古墳と同規模の築山が造られている。
==参考文献 == *『堺の文化財 百舌鳥古墳群』 堺市市長公室文化部文化財課 2008年 15項
==関連項目 == *[[百舌鳥古墳群]] *[[野中古墳]]
==外部リンク == [http://www.city.sakai.lg.jp/kofun/database/shichikan.html] 七観山古墳 堺市ホームページ 古墳データベース [[category:古墳]] [[category:円墳]] [[category:大阪府の古墳]] [[category:堺市の歴史]] [[category:堺区]]

==参考文献 == *『堺の文化財 百舌鳥古墳群』 堺市市長公室文化部文化財課 2008年 15項

'''七観山古墳'''（しちかんやまこふん）とは、[[大阪府]][[堺市]][[堺区]]旭ヶ丘中町4丁にかつて存在した[[古墳]]（[[円墳]]）で'''七観古墳'''（しちかんこふん）ともいう。[[百舌鳥古墳群]]に属しており、[[大正時代]]と太平洋戦争後に発掘され、大量の鉄製武器と甲冑が出土している。
== 概要== [[上石津ミサンザイ古墳]]（伝[[履中天皇]]陵古墳））の後円部周濠の北側に位置し、同古墳の[[陪塚]]とされる七観音古墳のすぐ北西に所在していた。[[墳丘]]は2段築に築かれ、径56m、高さ8m、周濠も存在した。[[葺石]]と[[埴輪]]があり、墳頂部には鰭付[[円筒埴輪]]の方形埴輪列が巡っていたようである。また、家、短甲、蓋（きぬがさ） 靫(ゆき)などの形象埴輪も出土している。1913年（大正2年）、1947年（昭和22年）と発掘または遺物採取が行なわれて遺物が出土しているが、1952年（昭和27年）には土取り工事によって消滅することになり、それに先だって緊急の[[発掘調査]]が行なわれている。主体部は遺物をおさめた[[粘土槨]]のような施設が3ヶ所検出されている。出土した遺物は、鉄製甲冑（衝角付冑7、短甲6以上など）、鉄製素環頭大刀、鉄刀（130前後）、蛇行状鉄剣、鉄剣、鉄鉾、鉄鏃（百数十）などの大量の武器、武具が出土している。また鉄斧、手斧、やりがんな、金銅製帯金具、それに[[鐙]]などの[[馬具]]も出土している。そのほかに、滑石製勾玉、剣形鉄製品が採集されている。馬具の鐙は日本国内で出土している物の中でもっとも古いものとされ、また帯金具は龍文透彫鍔板（りゅうもんすかしぼりかばん）に心葉形垂飾（しんようけいすいしょく）の付いた優品としてよく知られている。この古墳の性格として、大量の[[副葬品]]の埋納にもかかわらず、この[[古墳]]には遺体が埋葬されなかった可能性があり、陪塚と考えられる古墳のあり方を示す貴重な一例となっている。なお、七観山古墳の跡地は現在、[[大仙公園]]の敷地にとり込まれ、消滅した古墳と同規模の築山が造られている。
==参考文献 == *『堺の文化財 百舌鳥古墳群』 堺市市長公室文化部文化財課 2008年 15項

'''七観山古墳'''（しちかんやまこふん）とは、[[大阪府]][[堺市]][[堺区]]旭ヶ丘中町4丁にかつて存在した[[古墳]]（[[円墳]]）で'''七観古墳'''（しちかんこふん）ともいう。[[百舌鳥古墳群]]に属しており、[[大正時代]]と太平洋戦争後に発掘され、大量の鉄製武器と甲冑が出土している。
== 概要== [[上石津ミサンザイ古墳]]（伝[[履中天皇]]陵古墳））の後円部周濠の北側に位置し、同古墳の[[陪塚]]とされる七観音古墳のすぐ北西に所在していた。[[墳丘]]は2段築に築かれ、径56m、高さ8m、周濠も存在した。[[葺石]]と[[埴輪]]があり、墳頂部には鰭付[[円筒埴輪]]の方形埴輪列が巡っていたようである。また、家、短甲、蓋（きぬがさ） 靫(ゆき)などの形象埴輪も出土している。1913年（大正2年）、1947年（昭和22年）と発掘または遺物採取が行なわれて遺物が出土しているが、1952年（昭和27年）には土取り工事によって消滅することになり、それに先だって緊急の[[発掘調査]]が行なわれている。主体部は遺物をおさめた[[粘土槨]]のような施設が3ヶ所検出されている。 ==出土した遺物とその特色 == 出土した遺物は、鉄製甲冑（衝角付冑7、短甲6以上など）、鉄製素環頭大刀、鉄刀（130前後）、蛇行状鉄剣、鉄剣、鉄鉾、鉄鏃（百数十）などの大量の武器、武具が出土している。また鉄斧、手斧、やりがんな、金銅製帯金具、それに[[鐙]]などの[[馬具]]も出土している。そのほかに、滑石製勾玉、剣形鉄製品が採集されている。馬具の鐙は日本国内で出土している物の中でもっとも古いものとされている。また帯金具は龍文透彫鍔板（りゅうもんすかしぼりかばん）に心葉形垂飾（しんようけいすいしょく）の付いた優品としてよく知られており、[[韓国]][[慶尚北道]]の林堂洞7B号墳出土の帯金具と酷似し、同じ工人集団によって製作された可能性が指摘されている<ref>高田(2009)pp.59-61</ref>。日本列島で製作されたとする説と朝鮮半島説があるが、鍍金・金銅張り、鋳造・鍛造、たがねによる透彫などの彫金、鋲留技法など当時の中国・朝鮮の最先端の金工技術が駆使されているという。なお、この帯金具は短甲の腰の部分に巻きつけられた状態で出土しており、奈良県[[五条猫塚古墳]]や岡山県一本松古墳でも帯金具が同様の状態で出土しており、軍装の一部として扱われていたと考えられる。<ref>飯田(2011)pp.110-111</ref>。
この古墳の性格としては、大量の[[副葬品]]の埋納にもかかわらず、遺体が埋葬されなかった可能性があり、[[陪塚]]と考えられる古墳のあり方を示す貴重な一例となっている。なお、七観山古墳の跡地は現在、[[大仙公園]]の敷地にとり込まれ、消滅した古墳と同規模の築山が造られている。
==参考文献 == *『堺の文化財 百舌鳥古墳群』 堺市市長公室文化部文化財課 2008年 15項 *高田貫太「古墳時代装身具研究の意義」『季刊考古学 第106号』（株）雄山閣 57頁-61頁 *飯田浩光「七観古墳の帯金具-金工技術の精華」『百舌鳥・古市の陵墓古墳』[[近つ飛鳥博物館]]平成23年度秋期特別展図録 2011年 110－111頁

revision.comment: −[[Category:堺区]]; ±[[Category:堺市の歴史]]→[[Category:堺区の歴史]] ([[WP:HotCat|HotCat]]使用)
revision.text:
{{日本の古墳 |名称= 七観山古墳 |画像= |所在地= [[大阪府]][[堺市]][[堺区]]旭ヶ丘中町 |位置={{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|34|33|25|N|135|28|43|E|scale:20000}} |形状= [[円墳]] |規模=径56m |築造年代= [[5世紀]] |埋葬施設=木棺 |被葬者= |出土品=鉄剣 甲冑 馬具 |史跡指定= |特記事項= }}
'''七観山古墳'''（しちかんやまこふん）とは、[[大阪府]][[堺市]][[堺区]]旭ヶ丘中町4丁にかつて存在した[[古墳]]（[[円墳]]）で'''七観古墳'''（しちかんこふん）ともいう。[[百舌鳥古墳群]]に属しており、[[大正時代]]と太平洋戦争後に発掘され、大量の鉄製武器と甲冑が出土している。
== 概要== [[上石津ミサンザイ古墳]]（伝[[履中天皇]]陵古墳））の後円部周濠の北側に位置し、同古墳の[[陪塚]]とされる七観音古墳のすぐ北西に所在していた。[[墳丘]]は2段に築かれ、径56メートル、高さ8メートルあり、周濠も存在した。[[葺石]]と[[埴輪]]があり、墳頂部には鰭付[[円筒埴輪]]の方形埴輪列が巡っていたようである。また、家、短甲、蓋（きぬがさ） 靫(ゆき)などの形象埴輪も出土している。1913年（大正2年）、1947年（昭和22年）と発掘または遺物採取が行なわれて遺物が出土しているが、1952年（昭和27年）には土取り工事によって消滅することになり、それに先だって緊急の[[発掘調査]]が行なわれている。主体部は遺物をおさめた[[粘土槨]]のような施設が3ヶ所検出されている。 ==出土した遺物とその特色 == 出土した遺物は、鉄製甲冑（衝角付冑7、短甲6以上など）、鉄製素環頭大刀、鉄刀（130前後）、蛇行状鉄剣、鉄剣、鉄鉾、鉄鏃（百数十）などの大量の武器、武具が出土している。また鉄斧、手斧、やりがんな、金銅製帯金具、それに[[鐙]]などの[[馬具]]も出土している。そのほかに、滑石製勾玉、剣形鉄製品が採集されている。馬具の鐙は日本国内で出土している物の中でもっとも古いものとされている。また帯金具は龍文透彫鍔板（りゅうもんすかしぼりかばん）に心葉形垂飾（しんようけいすいしょく）の付いた優品としてよく知られており、[[韓国]][[慶尚北道]]の林堂洞7B号墳出土の帯金具と酷似し、同じ工人集団によって製作された可能性が指摘されている<ref>高田(2009)pp.59-61</ref>。日本列島で製作されたとする説と朝鮮半島説があるが、鍍金・金銅張り、鋳造・鍛造、たがねによる透彫などの彫金、鋲留技法など当時の中国・朝鮮の最先端の金工技術が駆使されているという。なお、この帯金具は短甲の腰の部分に巻きつけられた状態で出土しており、奈良県[[五条猫塚古墳]]や岡山県一本松古墳でも帯金具が同様の状態で出土しており、軍装の一部として扱われていたと考えられる。<ref>飯田(2011)pp.110-111</ref>。
この古墳の性格としては、大量の[[副葬品]]の埋納にもかかわらず、遺体が埋葬されなかった可能性があり、[[陪塚]]と考えられる古墳のあり方を示す貴重な一例となっている。なお、七観山古墳の跡地は現在、[[大仙公園]]の敷地にとり込まれ、消滅した古墳と同規模の築山が造られている。
==参考文献 == *『堺の文化財 百舌鳥古墳群』 堺市市長公室文化部文化財課 2008年 15項 *高田貫太「古墳時代装身具研究の意義」『季刊考古学 第106号』（株）雄山閣 57頁-61頁 *飯田浩光「七観古墳の帯金具-金工技術の精華」『百舌鳥・古市の陵墓古墳』[[近つ飛鳥博物館]]平成23年度秋期特別展図録 2011年 110－111頁
==脚注== {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist|2}}
==関連項目 == *[[百舌鳥古墳群]] *[[野中古墳]]
==外部リンク == *[http://www.city.sakai.lg.jp/kofun/database/shichikan.html デジタル古墳百科 古墳データベース 七観山古墳（七観古墳）] （堺市ウェブサイト）
{{デフォルトソート:しちかんやまこふん}} [[category:円墳]] [[category:百舌鳥古墳群|廃]] [[category:堺区の歴史]] [[category:和泉国]]

== 概要== [[上石津ミサンザイ古墳]]（伝[[履中天皇]]陵古墳））の後円部周濠の北側に位置し、同古墳の[[陪塚]]とされる七観音古墳のすぐ北西に所在していた。[[墳丘]]は2段に築かれ、径56メートル、高さ8メートルあり、周濠も存在した。[[葺石]]と[[埴輪]]があり、墳頂部には鰭付[[円筒埴輪]]の方形埴輪列が巡っていたようである。また、家、短甲、蓋（きぬがさ） 靫(ゆき)などの形象埴輪も出土している。1913年（大正2年）、1947年（昭和22年）と発掘または遺物採取が行なわれて遺物が出土しているが、1952年（昭和27年）には土取り工事によって消滅することになり、それに先だって緊急の[[発掘調査]]が行なわれている。主体部は遺物をおさめた[[粘土槨]]のような施設が3ヶ所検出されている。 ==出土した遺物とその特色 == 出土した遺物は、鉄製甲冑（衝角付冑7、短甲6以上など）、鉄製素環頭大刀、鉄刀（130前後）、蛇行状鉄剣、鉄剣、鉄鉾、鉄鏃（百数十）などの大量の武器、武具が出土している。また鉄斧、手斧、やりがんな、金銅製帯金具、それに[[鐙]]などの[[馬具]]も出土している。そのほかに、滑石製勾玉、剣形鉄製品が採集されている。馬具の鐙は日本国内で出土している物の中でもっとも古いものとされている。また帯金具は龍文透彫鍔板（りゅうもんすかしぼりかばん）に心葉形垂飾（しんようけいすいしょく）の付いた優品としてよく知られており、[[韓国]][[慶尚北道]]の林堂洞7B号墳出土の帯金具と酷似し、同じ工人集団によって製作された可能性が指摘されている<ref>高田(2009)pp.59-61</ref>。日本列島で製作されたとする説と朝鮮半島説があるが、鍍金・金銅張り、鋳造・鍛造、たがねによる透彫などの彫金、鋲留技法など当時の中国・朝鮮の最先端の金工技術が駆使されているという。なお、この帯金具は短甲の腰の部分に巻きつけられた状態で出土しており、奈良県[[五条猫塚古墳]]や岡山県一本松古墳でも帯金具が同様の状態で出土しており、軍装の一部として扱われていたと考えられる。<ref>飯田(2011)pp.110-111</ref>。
この古墳の性格としては、大量の[[副葬品]]の埋納にもかかわらず、遺体が埋葬されなかった可能性があり、[[陪塚]]と考えられる古墳のあり方を示す貴重な一例となっている。なお、七観山古墳の跡地は現在、[[大仙公園]]の敷地にとり込まれ、消滅した古墳と同規模の築山が造られている。
==参考文献 == *『堺の文化財 百舌鳥古墳群』 堺市市長公室文化部文化財課 2008年 15項 *高田貫太「古墳時代装身具研究の意義」『季刊考古学 第106号』（株）雄山閣 57頁-61頁 *飯田浩光「七観古墳の帯金具-金工技術の精華」『百舌鳥・古市の陵墓古墳』[[近つ飛鳥博物館]]平成23年度秋期特別展図録 2011年 110－111頁

==関連項目 == *[[百舌鳥古墳群]] *[[野中古墳]]
==外部リンク == *[http://www.city.sakai.lg.jp/kofun/database/shichikan.html デジタル古墳百科 古墳データベース 七観山古墳（七観古墳）] （堺市ウェブサイト）

{{日本の古墳 |名称= 七観山古墳 |画像= |所属=[[百舌鳥古墳群]] |所在地= [[大阪府]][[堺市]][[堺区]]旭ヶ丘中町 |位置={{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|34|33|25|N|135|28|43|E|scale:20000}} |形状= [[円墳]] |規模=径56m |築造年代= [[5世紀]] |埋葬施設=木棺 |被葬者= |出土品=鉄剣 甲冑 馬具 |指定文化財= |特記事項= }}
'''七観山古墳'''（しちかんやまこふん）とは、[[大阪府]][[堺市]][[堺区]]旭ヶ丘中町4丁にかつて存在した[[古墳]]（[[円墳]]）で'''七観古墳'''（しちかんこふん）ともいう。[[百舌鳥古墳群]]に属しており、[[大正時代]]と太平洋戦争後に発掘され、大量の鉄製武器と甲冑が出土している。
== 概要== [[上石津ミサンザイ古墳]]（伝[[履中天皇]]陵古墳））の後円部周濠の北側に位置し、同古墳の[[陪塚]]とされる七観音古墳のすぐ北西に所在していた。[[墳丘]]は2段に築かれ、径56メートル、高さ8メートルあり、周濠も存在した。[[葺石]]と[[埴輪]]があり、墳頂部には鰭付[[円筒埴輪]]の方形埴輪列が巡っていたようである。また、家、短甲、蓋（きぬがさ） 靫(ゆき)などの形象埴輪も出土している。1913年（大正2年）、1947年（昭和22年）と発掘または遺物採取が行なわれて遺物が出土しているが、1952年（昭和27年）には土取り工事によって消滅することになり、それに先だって緊急の[[発掘調査]]が行なわれている。主体部は遺物をおさめた[[粘土槨]]のような施設が3ヶ所検出されている。 ==出土した遺物とその特色 == 出土した遺物は、鉄製甲冑（衝角付冑7、短甲6以上など）、鉄製素環頭大刀、鉄刀（130前後）、蛇行状鉄剣、鉄剣、鉄鉾、鉄鏃（百数十）などの大量の武器、武具が出土している。また鉄斧、手斧、やりがんな、金銅製帯金具、それに[[鐙]]などの[[馬具]]も出土している。そのほかに、滑石製勾玉、剣形鉄製品が採集されている。馬具の鐙は日本国内で出土している物の中でもっとも古いものとされている。また帯金具は龍文透彫鍔板（りゅうもんすかしぼりかばん）に心葉形垂飾（しんようけいすいしょく）の付いた優品としてよく知られており、[[韓国]][[慶尚北道]]の林堂洞7B号墳出土の帯金具と酷似し、同じ工人集団によって製作された可能性が指摘されている<ref>高田(2009)pp.59-61</ref>。日本列島で製作されたとする説と朝鮮半島説があるが、鍍金・金銅張り、鋳造・鍛造、たがねによる透彫などの彫金、鋲留技法など当時の中国・朝鮮の最先端の金工技術が駆使されているという。なお、この帯金具は短甲の腰の部分に巻きつけられた状態で出土しており、奈良県[[五条猫塚古墳]]や岡山県一本松古墳でも帯金具が同様の状態で出土しており、軍装の一部として扱われていたと考えられる。<ref>飯田(2011)pp.110-111</ref>。
この古墳の性格としては、大量の[[副葬品]]の埋納にもかかわらず、遺体が埋葬されなかった可能性があり、[[陪塚]]と考えられる古墳のあり方を示す貴重な一例となっている。なお、七観山古墳の跡地は現在、[[大仙公園]]の敷地にとり込まれ、消滅した古墳と同規模の築山が造られている。

{{日本の古墳 |名称= 七観山古墳 |画像= |所属=[[百舌鳥古墳群]] |所在地= [[大阪府]][[堺市]][[堺区]]旭ヶ丘中町 |位置={{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|34|33|25|N|135|28|43|E|scale:20000}} |形状= [[円墳]] |規模=径56m |築造年代= [[5世紀]] |埋葬施設=木棺 |被葬者= |出土品=鉄剣 甲冑 馬具 |指定文化財= |特記事項= }}
'''七観山古墳'''（しちかんやまこふん）とは、[[大阪府]][[堺市]][[堺区]]旭ヶ丘中町4丁にかつて存在した[[古墳]]（[[円墳]]）で'''七観古墳'''（しちかんこふん）ともいう。[[百舌鳥古墳群]]に属しており、[[大正時代]]と[[太平洋戦争]]後に発掘され、大量の鉄製武器と[[甲冑]]が出土している。
== 概要== [[上石津ミサンザイ古墳]]（伝[[履中天皇]]陵古墳））の後円部周濠の北側に位置し、同古墳の[[陪塚]]とされる七観音古墳のすぐ北西に所在していた。[[墳丘]]は2段に築かれ、径56メートル、高さ8メートルあり、周濠も存在した。[[葺石]]と[[埴輪]]があり、墳頂部には鰭付[[円筒埴輪]]の方形埴輪列が巡っていたようである。また、家、短甲、蓋（きぬがさ） 靫(ゆき)などの形象埴輪も出土している。1913年（大正2年）、1947年（昭和22年）と発掘または遺物採取が行なわれて遺物が出土しているが、1952年（昭和27年）には土取り工事によって消滅することになり、それに先だって緊急の[[発掘調査]]が行なわれている。主体部は遺物をおさめた[[粘土槨]]のような施設が3ヶ所検出されている。 ==出土した遺物とその特色 == 出土した遺物は、鉄製甲冑（[[衝角付冑]]7、[[短甲]]6以上など）、鉄製素環頭[[大刀]]、[[直刀|鉄刀]]（130前後）、[[蛇行剣|蛇行状鉄剣]]、[[剣|鉄剣]]、[[矛|鉄鉾]]、[[鉄鏃]]（百数十）などの大量の武器、武具が出土している。このうち衝角付冑の中には、鉢の部分が動物の毛皮で作られたと見られる珍しいものも含まれている{{Sfn|堺市文化観光局文化部文化財課|2013|p=93-94}}{{Sfn|七観古墳研究会|2014|p=67-71}}。また鉄斧、手斧、やりがんな、金銅製帯金具、それに[[鐙]]などの[[馬具]]も出土している。そのほかに、滑石製[[勾玉]]、剣形鉄製品が採集されている。
馬具の鐙は日本国内で出土している物の中でもっとも古いものとされている。また帯金具は龍文透彫鍔板（りゅうもんすかしぼりかばん）に心葉形垂飾（しんようけいすいしょく）の付いた優品としてよく知られており、[[韓国]][[慶尚北道]]の林堂洞7B号墳出土の帯金具と酷似し、同じ工人集団によって製作された可能性が指摘されている<ref>高田(2009)pp.59-61</ref>。日本列島で製作されたとする説と朝鮮半島説があるが、鍍金・金銅張り、鋳造・鍛造、たがねによる透彫などの彫金、鋲留技法など当時の中国・朝鮮の最先端の金工技術が駆使されているという。なお、この帯金具は短甲の腰の部分に巻きつけられた状態で出土しており、奈良県[[五条猫塚古墳]]や岡山県一本松古墳でも帯金具が同様の状態で出土しており、軍装の一部として扱われていたと考えられる。<ref>飯田(2011)pp.110-111</ref>。
この古墳の性格としては、大量の[[副葬品]]の埋納にもかかわらず、遺体が埋葬されなかった可能性があり、[[陪塚]]と考えられる古墳のあり方を示す貴重な一例となっている。なお、七観山古墳の跡地は現在、[[大仙公園]]の敷地にとり込まれ、消滅した古墳と同規模の築山が造られている。

Created page with "{{Infobox Ancient Tomb Japan |name= Shichikanyama Kofun |image= |alias= Shichikan Kofun |part_of= {{ill|Mozu Kofun Group|ja|百舌鳥古墳群}} |location= {{ill|Sakai-ku|ja|堺区}}, {{ill|Sakai, Osaka|ja|堺市}}, Osaka Prefecture |coordinates= {{coord|34|33|25|N|135|28|43|E|scale:20000}} |type= {{ill|Round Kofun|ja|円墳}} |size= Diameter 56 m |construction_date= [[5th century]] |burial_facility= Wooden Coffin |buried_person= |artifacts= Iron swords, armor, horse gea..."
revision.text:
{{Infobox Ancient Tomb Japan |name= Shichikanyama Kofun |image= |alias= Shichikan Kofun |part_of= {{ill|Mozu Kofun Group|ja|百舌鳥古墳群}} |location= {{ill|Sakai-ku|ja|堺区}}, {{ill|Sakai, Osaka|ja|堺市}}, Osaka Prefecture |coordinates= {{coord|34|33|25|N|135|28|43|E|scale:20000}} |type= {{ill|Round Kofun|ja|円墳}} |size= Diameter 56 m |construction_date= [[5th century]] |burial_facility= Wooden Coffin |buried_person= |artifacts= Iron swords, armor, horse gear |designation1= Not designated |designation2= Not designated |notable_features= Destroyed Kofun |map2= {{Location map|Japan Osaka|label=Shichikanyama Kofun|lat_deg=34|lat_min=33|lat_sec=25.0|lon_deg=135|lon_min=28|lon_sec=43.0|mark=Round Kofun.svg|marksize=25|float=center|border=none|position=bottom|width=200|caption=Location within Osaka Prefecture}} }}
'''Shichikanyama Kofun''' (七観山古墳), also known as '''Shichikan Kofun''' (七観古墳), was a {{ill|round kofun|ja|円墳}} located in Asahigaoka Nakamachi 4-chome, {{ill|Sakai-ku|ja|堺区}}, {{ill|Sakai|ja|堺市}}, Osaka Prefecture. Part of the {{ill|Mozu Kofun Group|ja|百舌鳥古墳群}}, the mound was excavated during the Taishō period and again after World War II, yielding a large quantity of iron weapons and armor. It was destroyed in 1952 during land development.
== Overview ==
[[File:480220 Shichikanyama Kofun etc aerial.jpg|thumb|right|250px|Aerial view of Shichikanyama Kofun and surrounding kofun (February 1948). North is at the top. <small>{{National Air Photograph}}</small>]] The kofun was located just northwest of the {{ill|Kamizuka Misanzai Kofun|ja|上石津ミサンザイ古墳}} (attributed to {{ill|Emperor Richū|ja|履中天皇}}), near its northern moat. It was considered a {{ill|satellite tomb|ja|陪塚}} of the Shichikannon Kofun. The mound measured 56 meters in diameter and 8 meters in height, featuring moats, stone paving, and haniwa, including cylindrical haniwa and house-shaped haniwa. Artifacts from the mound suggest it was constructed in two tiers, with a clay burial chamber detected in three locations.
== Excavation and Findings ==
Artifacts recovered during excavations in 1913, 1947, and prior to its destruction in 1952 included: * **Armor and weapons**: Seven horned helmets (衝角付冑), six or more short-armored cuirasses (短甲), approximately 130 iron swords, serpentine iron swords (蛇行状鉄剣), iron spears, and over 100 iron arrowheads. * **Horse gear**: Ancient stirrups, regarded as the oldest found in Japan, and gilt bronze fittings for belts, likely imported or influenced by technology from the Korean Peninsula. * **Other items**: Magatama made from talc, iron axes, chisels, and ceremonial objects.
The bronze belt fittings featured exquisite craftsmanship, including openwork dragon motifs, which closely resemble items found in the {{ill|Imdang-dong Tomb No. 7B|ja|林堂洞7B号墳}} in Gyeongsangbuk-do, Korea. This suggests a connection between the craftsmen or shared cultural influences.
== Historical Significance ==
Despite the extensive grave goods, no human remains were identified in the tomb. This indicates the possibility that it served as a ceremonial site rather than a burial mound, providing valuable insight into the nature of {{ill|satellite tombs|ja|陪塚}} in the kofun period. Today, the site lies within the grounds of {{ill|Daisen Park|ja|大仙公園}}, where a reconstructed mound of similar size commemorates the original kofun.
== References == {{Reflist}}
== Further Reading == * {{Cite book|ja|author=Sakai City Cultural Affairs Department|title=Heritage of Sakai: The Mozu Kofun Group|publisher=Sakai City|year=2008|pages=15|ref=harv}} * {{Cite book|ja|last=Takada|first=Kanta|chapter=The Significance of Kofun-period Ornaments|title=Seasonal Archaeology No. 106|publisher=Yuzankaku|year=2009|pages=57-61|ref=harv}} * {{Cite book|ja|last=Iida|first=Hiromitsu|chapter=Belt Fittings of Shichikanyama Kofun|title=Autumn Special Exhibit Catalogue: The Tombs of Mozu and Furuichi|publisher=Chikatsu Asuka Museum|year=2011|pages=110-111|ref=harv}} * {{Cite book|ja|author=Sakai City Cultural Affairs Bureau|title=Black Weapons, Silver Armor: The Mozu Kofun Group and 5th-Century Conflicts|series=Sakai City Cultural Heritage Lecture Series Vol. 6|year=2013|ref=harv}} * {{Cite book|ja|author=Shichikan Kofun Research Group|title=Research on Shichikanyama Kofun: Reexamination of the Artifacts Recovered in 1947 and 1952|publisher=Shinyosha|year=2014|ref=harv}}
== Related Articles == * {{ill|Mozu Kofun Group|ja|百舌鳥古墳群}} * {{ill|Nochi Kofun|ja|野中古墳}}
== External Links == * [https://www.city.sakai.lg.jp/kanko/rekishi/dkofun/database/shichikanyama.html Digital Kofun Encyclopedia: Shichikanyama Kofun] (Sakai City Official Website)

{{Infobox Ancient Tomb Japan |name= Shichikanyama Kofun |image= |alias= Shichikan Kofun |part_of= {{ill|Mozu Kofun Group|ja|百舌鳥古墳群}} |location= {{ill|Sakai-ku|ja|堺区}}, {{ill|Sakai, Osaka|ja|堺市}}, Osaka Prefecture |coordinates= {{coord|34|33|25|N|135|28|43|E|scale:20000}} |type= {{ill|Round Kofun|ja|円墳}} |size= Diameter 56 m |construction_date= [[5th century]] |burial_facility= Wooden Coffin |buried_person= |artifacts= Iron swords, armor, horse gear |designation1= Not designated |designation2= Not designated |notable_features= Destroyed Kofun |map2= {{Location map|Japan Osaka|label=Shichikanyama Kofun|lat_deg=34|lat_min=33|lat_sec=25.0|lon_deg=135|lon_min=28|lon_sec=43.0|mark=Round Kofun.svg|marksize=25|float=center|border=none|position=bottom|width=200|caption=Location within Osaka Prefecture}} }}
'''Shichikanyama Kofun''' (七観山古墳), also known as '''Shichikan Kofun''' (七観古墳), was a {{ill|round kofun|ja|円墳}} located in Asahigaoka Nakamachi 4-chome, {{ill|Sakai-ku|ja|堺区}}, {{ill|Sakai|ja|堺市}}, Osaka Prefecture. Part of the {{ill|Mozu Kofun Group|ja|百舌鳥古墳群}}, the mound was excavated during the Taishō period and again after World War II, yielding a large quantity of iron weapons and armor. It was destroyed in 1952 during land development.
== Overview ==
[[File:480220 Shichikanyama Kofun etc aerial.jpg|thumb|right|250px|Aerial view of Shichikanyama Kofun and surrounding kofun (February 1948). North is at the top. <small>{{National Air Photograph}}</small>]] The kofun was located just northwest of the {{ill|Kamizuka Misanzai Kofun|ja|上石津ミサンザイ古墳}} (attributed to {{ill|Emperor Richū|ja|履中天皇}}), near its northern moat. It was considered a {{ill|satellite tomb|ja|陪塚}} of the Shichikannon Kofun. The mound measured 56 meters in diameter and 8 meters in height, featuring moats, stone paving, and haniwa, including cylindrical haniwa and house-shaped haniwa. Artifacts from the mound suggest it was constructed in two tiers, with a clay burial chamber detected in three locations.
== Excavation and Findings ==
Artifacts recovered during excavations in 1913, 1947, and prior to its destruction in 1952 included: * **Armor and weapons**: Seven horned helmets (衝角付冑), six or more short-armored cuirasses (短甲), approximately 130 iron swords, serpentine iron swords (蛇行状鉄剣), iron spears, and over 100 iron arrowheads. * **Horse gear**: Ancient stirrups, regarded as the oldest found in Japan, and gilt bronze fittings for belts, likely imported or influenced by technology from the Korean Peninsula. * **Other items**: Magatama made from talc, iron axes, chisels, and ceremonial objects.
The bronze belt fittings featured exquisite craftsmanship, including openwork dragon motifs, which closely resemble items found in the {{ill|Imdang-dong Tomb No. 7B|ja|林堂洞7B号墳}} in Gyeongsangbuk-do, Korea. This suggests a connection between the craftsmen or shared cultural influences.
== Historical Significance ==
Despite the extensive grave goods, no human remains were identified in the tomb. This indicates the possibility that it served as a ceremonial site rather than a burial mound, providing valuable insight into the nature of {{ill|satellite tombs|ja|陪塚}} in the kofun period. Today, the site lies within the grounds of {{ill|Daisen Park|ja|大仙公園}}, where a reconstructed mound of similar size commemorates the original kofun.
== References == {{Reflist}}
== Further Reading == * {{Cite book|ja|author=Sakai City Cultural Affairs Department|title=Heritage of Sakai: The Mozu Kofun Group|publisher=Sakai City|year=2008|pages=15|ref=harv}} * {{Cite book|ja|last=Takada|first=Kanta|chapter=The Significance of Kofun-period Ornaments|title=Seasonal Archaeology No. 106|publisher=Yuzankaku|year=2009|pages=57-61|ref=harv}} * {{Cite book|ja|last=Iida|first=Hiromitsu|chapter=Belt Fittings of Shichikanyama Kofun|title=Autumn Special Exhibit Catalogue: The Tombs of Mozu and Furuichi|publisher=Chikatsu Asuka Museum|year=2011|pages=110-111|ref=harv}} * {{Cite book|ja|author=Sakai City Cultural Affairs Bureau|title=Black Weapons, Silver Armor: The Mozu Kofun Group and 5th-Century Conflicts|series=Sakai City Cultural Heritage Lecture Series Vol. 6|year=2013|ref=harv}} * {{Cite book|ja|author=Shichikan Kofun Research Group|title=Research on Shichikanyama Kofun: Reexamination of the Artifacts Recovered in 1947 and 1952|publisher=Shinyosha|year=2014|ref=harv}}
== Related Articles == * {{ill|Mozu Kofun Group|ja|百舌鳥古墳群}} * {{ill|Nochi Kofun|ja|野中古墳}}

{{Infobox Ancient Tomb Japan |name= Shichikanyama Kofun |image= |alias= Shichikan Kofun |part_of= {{ill|Mozu Kofun Group|ja|百舌鳥古墳群}} |location= {{ill|Sakai-ku|ja|堺区}}, {{ill|Sakai, Osaka|ja|堺市}}, Osaka Prefecture |coordinates= {{coord|34|33|25|N|135|28|43|E|scale:20000}} |type= {{ill|Round Kofun|ja|円墳}} |size= Diameter 56 m |construction_date= [[5th century]] |burial_facility= Wooden Coffin |buried_person= |artifacts= Iron swords, armor, horse gear |designation1= Not designated |designation2= Not designated |notable_features= Destroyed Kofun |map2= {{location map|Japan Osaka|label=Shichikanyama Kofun|lat_deg=34|lat_min=33|lat_sec=25.0|lon_deg=135|lon_min=28|lon_sec=43.0|mark=Round Kofun.svg|marksize=25|float=center|border=none|position=bottom|width=200|caption=location within Osaka Prefecture}} }}
'''Shichikanyama Kofun''' (七観山古墳), also known as '''Shichikan Kofun''' (七観古墳), was a {{ill|Round Kofun|ja|円墳}} located in Asahigaoka Nakamachi 4-chome, {{ill|Sakai-ku|ja|堺区}}, {{ill|Sakai|ja|堺市}}, Osaka Prefecture. Part of the {{ill|Mozu Kofun Group|ja|百舌鳥古墳群}}, the mound was excavated during the Taishō period and again after World War II, yielding a large quantity of iron weapons and armor. It was destroyed in 1952 during land development.
== Overview ==
[[File:480220 Shichikanyama Kofun etc aerial.jpg|thumb|right|250px|Aerial view of Shichikanyama Kofun and surrounding kofun (February 1948). North is at the top. <small>{{National Air Photograph}}</small>]] The kofun was located just northwest of the {{ill|Kamizuka Misanzai Kofun|ja|上石津ミサンザイ古墳}} (attributed to {{ill|Emperor Richū|ja|履中天皇}}), near its northern moat. It was considered a {{ill|satellite tomb|ja|陪塚}} of the Shichikannon Kofun. The mound measured 56 meters in diameter and 8 meters in height, featuring moats, stone paving, and haniwa, including cylindrical haniwa and house-shaped haniwa. Artifacts from the mound suggest it was constructed in two tiers, with a clay burial chamber detected in three locations.
== Excavation and Findings ==
Artifacts recovered during excavations in 1913, 1947, and prior to its destruction in 1952 included: * '''Armor and weapons''': Seven horned helmets (衝角付冑), six or more short-armored cuirasses (短甲), approximately 130 iron swords, serpentine iron swords (蛇行状鉄剣), iron spears, and over 100 iron arrowheads. * '''Horse gear''': Ancient stirrups, regarded as the oldest found in Japan, and gilt bronze fittings for belts, likely imported or influenced by technology from the Korean Peninsula. * '''Other items''': Magatama made from talc, iron axes, chisels, and ceremonial objects.
The bronze belt fittings featured exquisite craftsmanship, including openwork dragon motifs, which closely resemble items found in the {{ill|Imdang-dong Tomb No. 7B|ja|林堂洞7B号墳}} in Gyeongsangbuk-do, Korea. This suggests a connection between the craftsmen or shared cultural influences.
== Historical Significance ==
Despite the extensive grave goods, no human remains were identified in the tomb. This indicates the possibility that it served as a ceremonial site rather than a burial mound, providing valuable insight into the nature of {{ill|satellite tomb|lt=satellite tombs|ja|陪塚}} in the kofun period. Today, the site lies within the grounds of {{ill|Daisen Park|ja|大仙公園}}, where a reconstructed mound of similar size commemorates the original kofun.
== References == {{Reflist}}
== Further Reading == * {{Cite book|ja|author=Sakai City Cultural Affairs Department|title=Heritage of Sakai: The Mozu Kofun Group|publisher=Sakai City|year=2008|pages=15|ref=harv}} * {{Cite book|ja|last=Takada|first=Kanta|chapter=The Significance of Kofun-period Ornaments|title=Seasonal Archaeology No. 106|publisher=Yuzankaku|year=2009|pages=57-61|ref=harv}} * {{Cite book|ja|last=Iida|first=Hiromitsu|chapter=Belt Fittings of Shichikanyama Kofun|title=Autumn Special Exhibit Catalogue: The Tombs of Mozu and Furuichi|publisher=Chikatsu Asuka Museum|year=2011|pages=110-111|ref=harv}} * {{Cite book|ja|author=Sakai City Cultural Affairs Bureau|title=Black Weapons, Silver Armor: The Mozu Kofun Group and 5th-Century Conflicts|series=Sakai City Cultural Heritage Lecture Series Vol. 6|year=2013|ref=harv}} * {{Cite book|ja|author=Shichikan Kofun Research Group|title=Research on Shichikanyama Kofun: Reexamination of the Artifacts Recovered in 1947 and 1952|publisher=Shinyosha|year=2014|ref=harv}}
== Related Articles == * {{ill|Mozu Kofun Group|ja|百舌鳥古墳群}} * {{ill|Nochi Kofun|ja|野中古墳}}
== External Links == * [https://www.city.sakai.lg.jp/kanko/rekishi/dkofun/database/shichikanyama.html Digital Kofun Encyclopedia: Shichikanyama Kofun] (Sakai City Official Website)

'''Shichikanyama Kofun''' (七観山古墳), also known as '''Shichikan Kofun''' (七観古墳), was a {{ill|Round Kofun|ja|円墳}} located in Asahigaoka Nakamachi 4-chome, {{ill|Sakai-ku|ja|堺区}}, {{ill|Sakai|ja|堺市}}, Osaka Prefecture. Part of the {{ill|Mozu Kofun Group|ja|百舌鳥古墳群}}, the mound was excavated during the Taishō period and again after World War II, yielding a large quantity of iron weapons and armor. It was destroyed in 1952 during land development.
== Overview ==
[[File:480220 Shichikanyama Kofun etc aerial.jpg|thumb|right|250px|Aerial view of Shichikanyama Kofun and surrounding kofun (February 1948). North is at the top. <small>{{National Air Photograph}}</small>]] The kofun was located just northwest of the {{ill|Kamizuka Misanzai Kofun|ja|上石津ミサンザイ古墳}} (attributed to {{ill|Emperor Richū|ja|履中天皇}}), near its northern moat. It was considered a {{ill|satellite tomb|ja|陪塚}} of the Shichikannon Kofun. The mound measured 56 meters in diameter and 8 meters in height, featuring moats, stone paving, and haniwa, including cylindrical haniwa and house-shaped haniwa. Artifacts from the mound suggest it was constructed in two tiers, with a clay burial chamber detected in three locations.

Artifacts recovered during excavations in 1913, 1947, and prior to its destruction in 1952 included: * '''Armor and weapons''': Seven horned helmets (衝角付冑), six or more short-armored cuirasses (短甲), approximately 130 iron swords, serpentine iron swords (蛇行状鉄剣), iron spears, and over 100 iron arrowheads. * '''Horse gear''': Ancient stirrups, regarded as the oldest found in Japan, and gilt bronze fittings for belts, likely imported or influenced by technology from the Korean Peninsula. * '''Other items''': Magatama made from talc, iron axes, chisels, and ceremonial objects.
The bronze belt fittings featured exquisite craftsmanship, including openwork dragon motifs, which closely resemble items found in the {{ill|Imdang-dong Tomb No. 7B|ja|林堂洞7B号墳}} in Gyeongsangbuk-do, Korea. This suggests a connection between the craftsmen or shared cultural influences.
== Historical Significance ==
Despite the extensive grave goods, no human remains were identified in the tomb. This indicates the possibility that it served as a ceremonial site rather than a burial mound, providing valuable insight into the nature of {{ill|satellite tomb|lt=satellite tombs|ja|陪塚}} in the kofun period. Today, the site lies within the grounds of {{ill|Daisen Park|ja|大仙公園}}, where a reconstructed mound of similar size commemorates the original kofun.
== References == {{Reflist}}
== Further Reading == * {{Cite book|ja|author=Sakai City Cultural Affairs Department|title=Heritage of Sakai: The Mozu Kofun Group|publisher=Sakai City|year=2008|pages=15|ref=harv}} * {{Cite book|ja|last=Takada|first=Kanta|chapter=The Significance of Kofun-period Ornaments|title=Seasonal Archaeology No. 106|publisher=Yuzankaku|year=2009|pages=57-61|ref=harv}} * {{Cite book|ja|last=Iida|first=Hiromitsu|chapter=Belt Fittings of Shichikanyama Kofun|title=Autumn Special Exhibit Catalogue: The Tombs of Mozu and Furuichi|publisher=Chikatsu Asuka Museum|year=2011|pages=110-111|ref=harv}} * {{Cite book|ja|author=Sakai City Cultural Affairs Bureau|title=Black Weapons, Silver Armor: The Mozu Kofun Group and 5th-Century Conflicts|series=Sakai City Cultural Heritage Lecture Series Vol. 6|year=2013|ref=harv}} * {{Cite book|ja|author=Shichikan Kofun Research Group|title=Research on Shichikanyama Kofun: Reexamination of the Artifacts Recovered in 1947 and 1952|publisher=Shinyosha|year=2014|ref=harv}}

North is at the top. <small>{{National Air Photograph}}</small>]] The kofun was located just northwest of the {{ill|Kamizuka Misanzai Kofun|ja|上石津ミサンザイ古墳}} (attributed to {{ill|Emperor Richū|ja|履中天皇}}), near its northern moat. It was considered a {{ill|satellite tomb|ja|陪塚}} of the Shichikannon Kofun. The mound measured 56 meters in diameter and 8 meters in height, featuring moats, stone paving, and haniwa, including cylindrical haniwa and house-shaped haniwa. Artifacts from the mound suggest it was constructed in two tiers, with a clay burial chamber detected in three locations.
== Excavation and Findings ==
Artifacts recovered during excavations in 1913, 1947, and prior to its destruction in 1952 included: * '''Armor and weapons''': Seven horned helmets (衝角付冑), six or more short-armored cuirasses (短甲), approximately 130 iron swords, serpentine iron swords (蛇行状鉄剣), iron spears, and over 100 iron arrowheads. * '''Horse gear''': Ancient stirrups, regarded as the oldest found in Japan, and gilt bronze fittings for belts, likely imported or influenced by technology from the Korean Peninsula. * '''Other items''': Magatama made from talc, iron axes, chisels, and ceremonial objects.
The bronze belt fittings featured exquisite craftsmanship, including openwork dragon motifs, which closely resemble items found in the {{ill|Imdang-dong Tomb No. 7B|ja|林堂洞7B号墳}} in Gyeongsangbuk-do, Korea. This suggests a connection between the craftsmen or shared cultural influences.
== Historical Significance ==
Despite the extensive grave goods, no human remains were identified in the tomb. This indicates the possibility that it served as a ceremonial site rather than a burial mound, providing valuable insight into the nature of {{ill|satellite tomb|lt=satellite tombs|ja|陪塚}} in the kofun period. Today, the site lies within the grounds of {{ill|Daisen Park|ja|大仙公園}}, where a reconstructed mound of similar size commemorates the original kofun.

{{Infobox ancient site |name= Shichikanyama Kofun |image= |alias= Shichikan Kofun |part_of= {{ill|Mozu Kofun Group|ja|百舌鳥古墳群}} |location= {{ill|Sakai-ku|ja|堺区}}, {{ill|Sakai, Osaka|ja|堺市}}, Osaka Prefecture |coordinates= {{coord|34|33|25|N|135|28|43|E|scale:20000}} |type= {{ill|Round Kofun|ja|円墳}} |size= Diameter 56 m |construction_date= [[5th century]] |burial_facility= Wooden Coffin |buried_person= |artifacts= Iron swords, armor, horse gear |designation1= Not designated |designation2= Not designated |notable_features= Destroyed Kofun |map2= {{location map|Japan Osaka|label=Shichikanyama Kofun|lat_deg=34|lat_min=33|lat_sec=25.0|lon_deg=135|lon_min=28|lon_sec=43.0|mark=Round Kofun.svg|marksize=25|float=center|border=none|position=bottom|width=200|caption=location within Osaka Prefecture}} }}
'''Shichikanyama Kofun''' (七観山古墳), also known as '''Shichikan Kofun''' (七観古墳), was a {{ill|Round Kofun|ja|円墳}} located in Asahigaoka Nakamachi 4-chome, {{ill|Sakai-ku|ja|堺区}}, {{ill|Sakai|ja|堺市}}, Osaka Prefecture. Part of the {{ill|Mozu Kofun Group|ja|百舌鳥古墳群}}, the mound was excavated during the Taishō period and again after World War II, yielding a large quantity of iron weapons and armor. It was destroyed in 1952 during land development.
== Overview ==
[[File:480220 Shichikanyama Kofun etc aerial.jpg|thumb|right|250px|Aerial view of Shichikanyama Kofun and surrounding kofun (February 1948). North is at the top. <small>{{National Air Photograph}}</small>]] The kofun was located just northwest of the {{ill|Kamizuka Misanzai Kofun|ja|上石津ミサンザイ古墳}} (attributed to {{ill|Emperor Richū|ja|履中天皇}}), near its northern moat. It was considered a {{ill|satellite tomb|ja|陪塚}} of the Shichikannon Kofun. The mound measured 56 meters in diameter and 8 meters in height, featuring moats, stone paving, and haniwa, including cylindrical haniwa and house-shaped haniwa. Artifacts from the mound suggest it was constructed in two tiers, with a clay burial chamber detected in three locations.
== Excavation and Findings ==
Artifacts recovered during excavations in 1913, 1947, and prior to its destruction in 1952 included: * '''Armor and weapons''': Seven horned helmets (衝角付冑), six or more short-armored cuirasses (短甲), approximately 130 iron swords, serpentine iron swords (蛇行状鉄剣), iron spears, and over 100 iron arrowheads. * '''Horse gear''': Ancient stirrups, regarded as the oldest found in Japan, and gilt bronze fittings for belts, likely imported or influenced by technology from the Korean Peninsula. * '''Other items''': Magatama made from talc, iron axes, chisels, and ceremonial objects.
The bronze belt fittings featured exquisite craftsmanship, including openwork dragon motifs, which closely resemble items found in the {{ill|Imdang-dong Tomb No. 7B|ja|林堂洞7B号墳}} in Gyeongsangbuk-do, Korea. This suggests a connection between the craftsmen or shared cultural influences.
== Historical Significance ==
Despite the extensive grave goods, no human remains were identified in the tomb. This indicates the possibility that it served as a ceremonial site rather than a burial mound, providing valuable insight into the nature of {{ill|satellite tomb|lt=satellite tombs|ja|陪塚}} in the kofun period. Today, the site lies within the grounds of {{ill|Daisen Park|ja|大仙公園}}, where a reconstructed mound of similar size commemorates the original kofun.
== References == {{Reflist}}
== Further Reading == * {{Cite book|ja|author=Sakai City Cultural Affairs Department|title=Heritage of Sakai: The Mozu Kofun Group|publisher=Sakai City|year=2008|pages=15|ref=harv}} * {{Cite book|ja|last=Takada|first=Kanta|chapter=The Significance of Kofun-period Ornaments|title=Seasonal Archaeology No. 106|publisher=Yuzankaku|year=2009|pages=57-61|ref=harv}} * {{Cite book|ja|last=Iida|first=Hiromitsu|chapter=Belt Fittings of Shichikanyama Kofun|title=Autumn Special Exhibit Catalogue: The Tombs of Mozu and Furuichi|publisher=Chikatsu Asuka Museum|year=2011|pages=110-111|ref=harv}} * {{Cite book|ja|author=Sakai City Cultural Affairs Bureau|title=Black Weapons, Silver Armor: The Mozu Kofun Group and 5th-Century Conflicts|series=Sakai City Cultural Heritage Lecture Series Vol. 6|year=2013|ref=harv}} * {{Cite book|ja|author=Shichikan Kofun Research Group|title=Research on Shichikanyama Kofun: Reexamination of the Artifacts Recovered in 1947 and 1952|publisher=Shinyosha|year=2014|ref=harv}}
== Related Articles == * {{ill|Mozu Kofun Group|ja|百舌鳥古墳群}} * {{ill|Nochi Kofun|ja|野中古墳}}
== External Links == * [https://www.city.sakai.lg.jp/kanko/rekishi/dkofun/database/shichikanyama.html Digital Kofun Encyclopedia: Shichikanyama Kofun] (Sakai City Official Website)

revision.text:
{{Infobox ancient site |name= Shichikanyama Kofun |image= |alias= Shichikan Kofun |part_of= {{ill|Mozu Kofun Group|ja|百舌鳥古墳群}} |location= {{ill|Sakai-ku|ja|堺区}}, {{ill|Sakai, Osaka|ja|堺市}}, Osaka Prefecture |coordinates= {{coord|34|33|25|N|135|28|43|E|scale:20000}} |type= {{ill|Round Kofun|ja|円墳}} |size= Diameter 56 m |construction_date= [[5th century]] |burial_facility= Wooden Coffin |buried_person= |artifacts= Iron swords, armor, horse gear |designation1= Not designated |designation2= Not designated |notable_features= Destroyed Kofun |map2= {{location map|Japan Osaka|label=Shichikanyama Kofun|lat_deg=34|lat_min=33|lat_sec=25.0|lon_deg=135|lon_min=28|lon_sec=43.0|mark=Round Kofun.svg|marksize=25|float=center|border=none|position=bottom|width=200|caption=location within Osaka Prefecture}} }}
'''Shichikanyama Kofun''' (七観山古墳), also known as '''Shichikan Kofun''' (七観古墳), was a {{ill|Round Kofun|ja|円墳}} located in Asahigaoka Nakamachi 4-chome, {{ill|Sakai-ku|ja|堺区}}, {{ill|Sakai|ja|堺市}}, Osaka Prefecture. Part of the {{ill|Mozu Kofun Group|ja|百舌鳥古墳群}}, the mound was excavated during the Taishō period and again after World War II, yielding a large quantity of iron weapons and armor. It was destroyed in 1952 during land development.
== Overview ==
[[File:480220 Shichikanyama Kofun etc aerial.jpg|thumb|right|250px|Aerial view of Shichikanyama Kofun and surrounding kofun (February 1948). North is at the top. <small>{{National Air Photograph}}</small>]] The kofun was located just northwest of the {{ill|Kamizuka Misanzai Kofun|ja|上石津ミサンザイ古墳}} (attributed to {{ill|Emperor Richū|ja|履中天皇}}), near its northern moat. It was considered a {{ill|satellite tomb|ja|陪塚}} of the Shichikannon Kofun. The mound measured 56 meters in diameter and 8 meters in height, featuring moats, stone paving, and haniwa, including cylindrical haniwa and house-shaped haniwa. Artifacts from the mound suggest it was constructed in two tiers, with a clay burial chamber detected in three locations.
== Excavation and Findings ==
Artifacts recovered during excavations in 1913, 1947, and prior to its destruction in 1952 included: * '''Armor and weapons''': Seven horned helmets (衝角付冑), six or more short-armored cuirasses (短甲), approximately 130 iron swords, serpentine iron swords (蛇行状鉄剣), iron spears, and over 100 iron arrowheads. * '''Horse gear''': Ancient stirrups, regarded as the oldest found in Japan, and gilt bronze fittings for belts, likely imported or influenced by technology from the Korean Peninsula. * '''Other items''': Magatama made from talc, iron axes, chisels, and ceremonial objects.
The bronze belt fittings featured exquisite craftsmanship, including openwork dragon motifs, which closely resemble items found in the {{ill|Imdang-dong Tomb No. 7B|ja|林堂洞7B号墳}} in Gyeongsangbuk-do, Korea. This suggests a connection between the craftsmen or shared cultural influences.
== Historical Significance ==
Despite the extensive grave goods, no human remains were identified in the tomb. This indicates the possibility that it served as a ceremonial site rather than a burial mound, providing valuable insight into the nature of {{ill|satellite tomb|lt=satellite tombs|ja|陪塚}} in the kofun period. Today, the site lies within the grounds of {{ill|Daisen Park|ja|大仙公園}}, where a reconstructed mound of similar size commemorates the original kofun.
== References == {{Reflist}}
== Further Reading == * {{Cite book|ja|author=Sakai City Cultural Affairs Department|title=Heritage of Sakai: The Mozu Kofun Group|publisher=Sakai City|year=2008|pages=15|ref=harv}} * {{Cite book|ja|last=Takada|first=Kanta|chapter=The Significance of Kofun-period Ornaments|title=Seasonal Archaeology No. 106|publisher=Yuzankaku|year=2009|pages=57-61|ref=harv}} * {{Cite book|ja|last=Iida|first=Hiromitsu|chapter=Belt Fittings of Shichikanyama Kofun|title=Autumn Special Exhibit Catalogue: The Tombs of Mozu and Furuichi|publisher=Chikatsu Asuka Museum|year=2011|pages=110-111|ref=harv}} * {{Cite book|ja|author=Sakai City Cultural Affairs Bureau|title=Black Weapons, Silver Armor: The Mozu Kofun Group and 5th-Century Conflicts|series=Sakai City Cultural Heritage Lecture Series Vol. 6|year=2013|ref=harv}} * {{Cite book|ja|author=Shichikan Kofun Research Group|title=Research on Shichikanyama Kofun: Reexamination of the Artifacts Recovered in 1947 and 1952|publisher=Shinyosha|year=2014|ref=harv}}
== Related Articles == * {{ill|Mozu Kofun Group|ja|百舌鳥古墳群}} * {{ill|Nochi Kofun|ja|野中古墳}}
== External Links == * [https://www.city.sakai.lg.jp/kanko/rekishi/dkofun/database/shichikanyama.html Digital Kofun Encyclopedia: Shichikanyama Kofun] (Sakai City Official Website)

'''Shichikanyama Kofun''' (七観山古墳), also known as '''Shichikan Kofun''' (七観古墳), was a {{ill|Round Kofun|ja|円墳}} located in Asahigaoka Nakamachi 4-chome, {{ill|Sakai-ku|ja|堺区}}, {{ill|Sakai|ja|堺市}}, Osaka Prefecture. Part of the {{ill|Mozu Kofun Group|ja|百舌鳥古墳群}}, the mound was excavated during the Taishō period and again after World War II, yielding a large quantity of iron weapons and armor. It was destroyed in 1952 during land development.
== Overview ==
[[File:480220 Shichikanyama Kofun etc aerial.jpg|thumb|right|250px|Aerial view of Shichikanyama Kofun and surrounding kofun (February 1948). North is at the top. <small>{{National Air Photograph}}</small>]] The kofun was located just northwest of the {{ill|Kamizuka Misanzai Kofun|ja|上石津ミサンザイ古墳}} (attributed to {{ill|Emperor Richū|ja|履中天皇}}), near its northern moat. It was considered a {{ill|satellite tomb|ja|陪塚}} of the Shichikannon Kofun. The mound measured 56 meters in diameter and 8 meters in height, featuring moats, stone paving, and haniwa, including cylindrical haniwa and house-shaped haniwa. Artifacts from the mound suggest it was constructed in two tiers, with a clay burial chamber detected in three locations.
== Excavation and Findings ==
Artifacts recovered during excavations in 1913, 1947, and prior to its destruction in 1952 included: * '''Armor and weapons''': Seven horned helmets (衝角付冑), six or more short-armored cuirasses (短甲), approximately 130 iron swords, serpentine iron swords (蛇行状鉄剣), iron spears, and over 100 iron arrowheads. * '''Horse gear''': Ancient stirrups, regarded as the oldest found in Japan, and gilt bronze fittings for belts, likely imported or influenced by technology from the Korean Peninsula. * '''Other items''': Magatama made from talc, iron axes, chisels, and ceremonial objects.
The bronze belt fittings featured exquisite craftsmanship, including openwork dragon motifs, which closely resemble items found in the {{ill|Imdang-dong Tomb No. 7B|ja|林堂洞7B号墳}} in Gyeongsangbuk-do, Korea. This suggests a connection between the craftsmen or shared cultural influences.
== Historical Significance ==
Despite the extensive grave goods, no human remains were identified in the tomb. This indicates the possibility that it served as a ceremonial site rather than a burial mound, providing valuable insight into the nature of {{ill|satellite tomb|lt=satellite tombs|ja|陪塚}} in the kofun period. Today, the site lies within the grounds of {{ill|Daisen Park|ja|大仙公園}}, where a reconstructed mound of similar size commemorates the original kofun.
== References == {{Reflist}}
== Further Reading == * {{Cite book|ja|author=Sakai City Cultural Affairs Department|title=Heritage of Sakai: The Mozu Kofun Group|publisher=Sakai City|year=2008|pages=15|ref=harv}} * {{Cite book|ja|last=Takada|first=Kanta|chapter=The Significance of Kofun-period Ornaments|title=Seasonal Archaeology No. 106|publisher=Yuzankaku|year=2009|pages=57-61|ref=harv}} * {{Cite book|ja|last=Iida|first=Hiromitsu|chapter=Belt Fittings of Shichikanyama Kofun|title=Autumn Special Exhibit Catalogue: The Tombs of Mozu and Furuichi|publisher=Chikatsu Asuka Museum|year=2011|pages=110-111|ref=harv}} * {{Cite book|ja|author=Sakai City Cultural Affairs Bureau|title=Black Weapons, Silver Armor: The Mozu Kofun Group and 5th-Century Conflicts|series=Sakai City Cultural Heritage Lecture Series Vol. 6|year=2013|ref=harv}} * {{Cite book|ja|author=Shichikan Kofun Research Group|title=Research on Shichikanyama Kofun: Reexamination of the Artifacts Recovered in 1947 and 1952|publisher=Shinyosha|year=2014|ref=harv}}
== Related Articles == * {{ill|Mozu Kofun Group|ja|百舌鳥古墳群}} * {{ill|Nochi Kofun|ja|野中古墳}}
== External Links == * [https://www.city.sakai.lg.jp/kanko/rekishi/dkofun/database/shichikanyama.html Digital Kofun Encyclopedia: Shichikanyama Kofun] (Sakai City Official Website)

{{Infobox ancient site |name= Shichikanyama Kofun |image= |alias= Shichikan Kofun |part_of= {{ill|Mozu Kofun Group|ja|百舌鳥古墳群}} |location= {{ill|Sakai-ku|ja|堺区}}, {{ill|Sakai, Osaka|ja|堺市}}, Osaka Prefecture |coordinates= {{coord|34|33|25|N|135|28|43|E|scale:20000}} |type= {{ill|Round Kofun|ja|円墳}} |size= Diameter 56 m |construction_date= [[5th century]] |burial_facility= Wooden Coffin |buried_person= |artifacts= Iron swords, armor, horse gear |designation1= Not designated |designation2= Not designated |notable_features= Destroyed Kofun |map2= {{location map|Japan Osaka|label=Shichikanyama Kofun|lat_deg=34|lat_min=33|lat_sec=25.0|lon_deg=135|lon_min=28|lon_sec=43.0|mark=Round Kofun.svg|marksize=25|float=center|border=none|position=bottom|width=200|caption=location within Osaka Prefecture}} }}
'''Shichikanyama Kofun''' (七観山古墳), also known as '''Shichikan Kofun''' (七観古墳), was a {{ill|Round Kofun|ja|円墳}} located in Asahigaoka Nakamachi 4-chome, {{ill|Sakai-ku|ja|堺区}}, {{ill|Sakai|ja|堺市}}, Osaka Prefecture. Part of the {{ill|Mozu Kofun Group|ja|百舌鳥古墳群}}, the mound was excavated during the Taishō period and again after World War II, yielding a large quantity of iron weapons and armor. It was destroyed in 1952 during land development.
== Overview ==
[[File:480220 Shichikanyama Kofun etc aerial.jpg|thumb|right|250px|Aerial view of Shichikanyama Kofun and surrounding kofun (February 1948). North is at the top. <small>{{National Air Photograph}}</small>]] The kofun was located just northwest of the {{ill|Kamizuka Misanzai Kofun|ja|上石津ミサンザイ古墳}} (attributed to {{ill|Emperor Richū|ja|履中天皇}}), near its northern moat. It was considered a {{ill|satellite tomb|ja|陪塚}} of the Shichikannon Kofun. The mound measured 56 meters in diameter and 8 meters in height, featuring moats, stone paving, and haniwa, including cylindrical haniwa and house-shaped haniwa. Artifacts from the mound suggest it was constructed in two tiers, with a clay burial chamber detected in three locations.
== Excavation and Findings ==
Artifacts recovered during excavations in 1913, 1947, and prior to its destruction in 1952 included: * '''Armor and weapons''': Seven horned helmets (衝角付冑), six or more short-armored cuirasses (短甲), approximately 130 iron swords, serpentine iron swords (蛇行状鉄剣), iron spears, and over 100 iron arrowheads. * '''Horse gear''': Ancient stirrups, regarded as the oldest found in Japan, and gilt bronze fittings for belts, likely imported or influenced by technology from the Korean Peninsula. * '''Other items''': Magatama made from talc, iron axes, chisels, and ceremonial objects.
The bronze belt fittings featured exquisite craftsmanship, including openwork dragon motifs, which closely resemble items found in the {{ill|Imdang-dong Tomb No. 7B|ja|林堂洞7B号墳}} in Gyeongsangbuk-do, Korea. This suggests a connection between the craftsmen or shared cultural influences.
== Historical Significance ==
Despite the extensive grave goods, no human remains were identified in the tomb. This indicates the possibility that it served as a ceremonial site rather than a burial mound, providing valuable insight into the nature of {{ill|satellite tomb|lt=satellite tombs|ja|陪塚}} in the kofun period. Today, the site lies within the grounds of {{ill|Daisen Park|ja|大仙公園}}, where a reconstructed mound of similar size commemorates the original kofun.

{{Infobox ancient site |name= Shichikanyama Kofun |image= |alias= Shichikan Kofun |part_of= {{ill|Mozu Kofun Group|ja|百舌鳥古墳群}} |location= {{ill|Sakai-ku|ja|堺区}}, {{ill|Sakai, Osaka|ja|堺市}}, Osaka Prefecture |coordinates= {{coord|34|33|25|N|135|28|43|E|scale:20000}} |type= {{ill|Round Kofun|ja|円墳}} |size= Diameter 56 m |construction_date= [[5th century]] |burial_facility= Wooden Coffin |buried_person= |artifacts= Iron swords, armor, horse gear |designation1= Not designated |designation2= Not designated |notable_features= Destroyed Kofun |map2= {{location map|Japan Osaka|label=Shichikanyama Kofun|lat_deg=34|lat_min=33|lat_sec=25.0|lon_deg=135|lon_min=28|lon_sec=43.0|mark=Round Kofun.svg|marksize=25|float=center|border=none|position=bottom|width=200|caption=location within Osaka Prefecture}} }}
'''Shichikanyama Kofun''' (七観山古墳), also known as '''Shichikan Kofun''' (七観古墳), was a {{ill|Round Kofun|ja|円墳}} located in Asahigaoka Nakamachi 4-chome, {{ill|Sakai-ku|ja|堺区}}, {{ill|Sakai|ja|堺市}}, Osaka Prefecture. Part of the {{ill|Mozu Kofun Group|ja|百舌鳥古墳群}}, the mound was excavated during the Taishō period and again after World War II, yielding a large quantity of iron weapons and armor. It was destroyed in 1952 during land development.
== Overview ==
[[File:480220 Shichikanyama Kofun etc aerial.jpg|thumb|right|250px|Aerial view of Shichikanyama Kofun and surrounding kofun (February 1948). North is at the top. <small>{{National Air Photograph}}</small>]] The kofun was located just northwest of the {{ill|Kamizuka Misanzai Kofun|ja|上石津ミサンザイ古墳}} (attributed to {{ill|Emperor Richū|ja|履中天皇}}), near its northern moat. It was considered a {{ill|satellite tomb|ja|陪塚}} of the Shichikannon Kofun. The mound measured 56 meters in diameter and 8 meters in height, featuring moats, stone paving, and haniwa, including cylindrical haniwa and house-shaped haniwa. Artifacts from the mound suggest it was constructed in two tiers, with a clay burial chamber detected in three locations.
== Excavation and Findings ==
Artifacts recovered during excavations in 1913, 1947, and prior to its destruction in 1952 included: * '''Armor and weapons''': Seven horned helmets (衝角付冑), six or more short-armored cuirasses (短甲), approximately 130 iron swords, serpentine iron swords (蛇行状鉄剣), iron spears, and over 100 iron arrowheads. * '''Horse gear''': Ancient stirrups, regarded as the oldest found in Japan, and gilt bronze fittings for belts, likely imported or influenced by technology from the Korean Peninsula. * '''Other items''': Magatama made from talc, iron axes, chisels, and ceremonial objects.
The bronze belt fittings featured exquisite craftsmanship, including openwork dragon motifs, which closely resemble items found in the {{ill|Imdang-dong Tomb No. 7B|ja|林堂洞7B号墳}} in Gyeongsangbuk-do, Korea. This suggests a connection between the craftsmen or shared cultural influences.
== Historical Significance ==
Despite the extensive grave goods, no human remains were identified in the tomb. This indicates the possibility that it served as a ceremonial site rather than a burial mound, providing valuable insight into the nature of {{ill|satellite tomb|lt=satellite tombs|ja|陪塚}} in the kofun period. Today, the site lies within the grounds of {{ill|Daisen Park|ja|大仙公園}}, where a reconstructed mound of similar size commemorates the original kofun.
== References == {{Reflist}}
== Further Reading == * {{Cite book|ja|author=Sakai City Cultural Affairs Department|title=Heritage of Sakai: The Mozu Kofun Group|publisher=Sakai City|year=2008|pages=15|ref=harv}} * {{Cite book|ja|last=Takada|first=Kanta|chapter=The Significance of Kofun-period Ornaments|title=Seasonal Archaeology No. 106|publisher=Yuzankaku|year=2009|pages=57-61|ref=harv}} * {{Cite book|ja|last=Iida|first=Hiromitsu|chapter=Belt Fittings of Shichikanyama Kofun|title=Autumn Special Exhibit Catalogue: The Tombs of Mozu and Furuichi|publisher=Chikatsu Asuka Museum|year=2011|pages=110-111|ref=harv}} * {{Cite book|ja|author=Sakai City Cultural Affairs Bureau|title=Black Weapons, Silver Armor: The Mozu Kofun Group and 5th-Century Conflicts|series=Sakai City Cultural Heritage Lecture Series Vol. 6|year=2013|ref=harv}} * {{Cite book|ja|author=Shichikan Kofun Research Group|title=Research on Shichikanyama Kofun: Reexamination of the Artifacts Recovered in 1947 and 1952|publisher=Shinyosha|year=2014|ref=harv}}
== Related Articles == * {{ill|Mozu Kofun Group|ja|百舌鳥古墳群}} * {{ill|Nochi Kofun|ja|野中古墳}}
== External Links == * [https://www.city.sakai.lg.jp/kanko/rekishi/dkofun/database/shichikanyama.html Digital Kofun Encyclopedia: Shichikanyama Kofun] (Sakai City Official Website)

{{Infobox ancient site |name= Shichikanyama Kofun |image= |alias= Shichikan Kofun |part_of= {{ill|Mozu Kofun Group|ja|百舌鳥古墳群}} |location= {{ill|Sakai-ku|ja|堺区}}, {{ill|Sakai, Osaka|ja|堺市}}, Osaka Prefecture |coordinates= {{coord|34|33|25|N|135|28|43|E|scale:20000}} |type= {{ill|Round Kofun|ja|円墳}} |size= Diameter 56 m |construction_date= [[5th century]] |burial_facility= Wooden Coffin |buried_person= |artifacts= Iron swords, armor, horse gear |designation1= Not designated |designation2= Not designated |notable_features= Destroyed Kofun |map2= {{location map|Japan Osaka|label=Shichikanyama Kofun|lat_deg=34|lat_min=33|lat_sec=25.0|lon_deg=135|lon_min=28|lon_sec=43.0|mark=Round Kofun.svg|marksize=25|float=center|border=none|position=bottom|width=200|caption=location within Osaka Prefecture}} }}
'''Shichikanyama Kofun''' (七観山古墳), also known as '''Shichikan Kofun''' (七観古墳), was a {{ill|Round Kofun|ja|円墳}} located in Asahigaoka Nakamachi 4-chome, {{ill|Sakai-ku|ja|堺区}}, {{ill|Sakai|ja|堺市}}, Osaka Prefecture. Part of the {{ill|Mozu Kofun Group|ja|百舌鳥古墳群}}, the mound was excavated during the Taishō period and again after World War II, yielding a large quantity of iron weapons and armor. It was destroyed in 1952 during land development.
== Overview ==
[[File:480220 Shichikanyama Kofun etc aerial.jpg|thumb|right|250px|Aerial view of Shichikanyama Kofun and surrounding kofun (February 1948). North is at the top. <small>{{National Air Photograph}}</small>]] The kofun was located just northwest of the {{ill|Kamizuka Misanzai Kofun|ja|上石津ミサンザイ古墳}} (attributed to {{ill|Emperor Richū|ja|履中天皇}}), near its northern moat. It was considered a {{ill|satellite tomb|ja|陪塚}} of the Shichikannon Kofun. The mound measured 56 meters in diameter and 8 meters in height, featuring moats, stone paving, and haniwa, including cylindrical haniwa and house-shaped haniwa. Artifacts from the mound suggest it was constructed in two tiers, with a clay burial chamber detected in three locations.
== Excavation and Findings ==
Artifacts recovered during excavations in 1913, 1947, and prior to its destruction in 1952 included: * '''Armor and weapons''': Seven horned helmets (衝角付冑), six or more short-armored cuirasses (短甲), approximately 130 iron swords, serpentine iron swords (蛇行状鉄剣), iron spears, and over 100 iron arrowheads. * '''Horse gear''': Ancient stirrups, regarded as the oldest found in Japan, and gilt bronze fittings for belts, likely imported or influenced by technology from the Korean Peninsula. * '''Other items''': Magatama made from talc, iron axes, chisels, and ceremonial objects.
The bronze belt fittings featured exquisite craftsmanship, including openwork dragon motifs, which closely resemble items found in the {{ill|Imdang-dong Tomb No. 7B|ja|林堂洞7B号墳}} in Gyeongsangbuk-do, Korea. This suggests a connection between the craftsmen or shared cultural influences.
== Historical Significance ==
Despite the extensive grave goods, no human remains were identified in the tomb. This indicates the possibility that it served as a ceremonial site rather than a burial mound, providing valuable insight into the nature of {{ill|satellite tomb|lt=satellite tombs|ja|陪塚}} in the kofun period. Today, the site lies within the grounds of {{ill|Daisen Park|ja|大仙公園}}, where a reconstructed mound of similar size commemorates the original kofun.
== References == {{Reflist}}
== Further Reading == * {{Cite book|ja|author=Sakai City Cultural Affairs Department|title=Heritage of Sakai: The Mozu Kofun Group|publisher=Sakai City|year=2008|pages=15|ref=harv}} * {{Cite book|ja|last=Takada|first=Kanta|chapter=The Significance of Kofun-period Ornaments|title=Seasonal Archaeology No. 106|publisher=Yuzankaku|year=2009|pages=57-61|ref=harv}} * {{Cite book|ja|last=Iida|first=Hiromitsu|chapter=Belt Fittings of Shichikanyama Kofun|title=Autumn Special Exhibit Catalogue: The Tombs of Mozu and Furuichi|publisher=Chikatsu Asuka Museum|year=2011|pages=110-111|ref=harv}} * {{Cite book|ja|author=Sakai City Cultural Affairs Bureau|title=Black Weapons, Silver Armor: The Mozu Kofun Group and 5th-Century Conflicts|series=Sakai City Cultural Heritage Lecture Series Vol. 6|year=2013|ref=harv}} * {{Cite book|ja|author=Shichikan Kofun Research Group|title=Research on Shichikanyama Kofun: Reexamination of the Artifacts Recovered in 1947 and 1952|publisher=Shinyosha|year=2014|ref=harv}}

{{Infobox ancient site |name= Shichikanyama Kofun |image= |alias= Shichikan Kofun |part_of= {{ill|Mozu Kofun Group|ja|百舌鳥古墳群}} |location= {{ill|Sakai-ku|ja|堺区}}, {{ill|Sakai, Osaka|ja|堺市}}, Osaka Prefecture |coordinates= {{coord|34|33|25|N|135|28|43|E|scale:20000}} |type= {{ill|Round Kofun|ja|円墳}} |size= Diameter 56 m |construction_date= [[5th century]] |burial_facility= Wooden Coffin |buried_person= |artifacts= Iron swords, armor, horse gear |designation1= Not designated |designation2= Not designated |notable_features= Destroyed Kofun |map2= {{location map|Japan Osaka|label=Shichikanyama Kofun|lat_deg=34|lat_min=33|lat_sec=25.0|lon_deg=135|lon_min=28|lon_sec=43.0|mark=Round Kofun.svg|marksize=25|float=center|border=none|position=bottom|width=200|caption=location within Osaka Prefecture}} }}
'''Shichikanyama Kofun''' (七観山古墳), also known as '''Shichikan Kofun''' (七観古墳), was a {{ill|Round Kofun|ja|円墳}} located in Asahigaoka Nakamachi 4-chome, {{ill|Sakai-ku|ja|堺区}}, {{ill|Sakai|ja|堺市}}, Osaka Prefecture. Part of the {{ill|Mozu Kofun Group|ja|百舌鳥古墳群}}, the mound was excavated during the Taishō period and again after World War II, yielding a large quantity of iron weapons and armor. It was destroyed in 1952 during land development.
== Overview ==
[[File:480220 Shichikanyama Kofun etc aerial.jpg|thumb|right|250px|Aerial view of Shichikanyama Kofun and surrounding kofun (February 1948). North is at the top. <small>{{National Air Photograph}}</small>]] The kofun was located just northwest of the {{ill|Kamizuka Misanzai Kofun|ja|上石津ミサンザイ古墳}} (attributed to {{ill|Emperor Richū|ja|履中天皇}}), near its northern moat. It was considered a {{ill|satellite tomb|ja|陪塚}} of the Shichikannon Kofun. The mound measured 56 meters in diameter and 8 meters in height, featuring moats, stone paving, and haniwa, including cylindrical haniwa and house-shaped haniwa. Artifacts from the mound suggest it was constructed in two tiers, with a clay burial chamber detected in three locations.
== Excavation and Findings ==
Artifacts recovered during excavations in 1913, 1947, and prior to its destruction in 1952 included: * '''Armor and weapons''': Seven horned helmets (衝角付冑), six or more short-armored cuirasses (短甲), approximately 130 iron swords, serpentine iron swords (蛇行状鉄剣), iron spears, and over 100 iron arrowheads. * '''Horse gear''': Ancient stirrups, regarded as the oldest found in Japan, and gilt bronze fittings for belts, likely imported or influenced by technology from the Korean Peninsula. * '''Other items''': Magatama made from talc, iron axes, chisels, and ceremonial objects.
The bronze belt fittings featured exquisite craftsmanship, including openwork dragon motifs, which closely resemble items found in the {{ill|Imdang-dong Tomb No. 7B|ja|林堂洞7B号墳}} in Gyeongsangbuk-do, Korea. This suggests a connection between the craftsmen or shared cultural influences.
== Historical Significance ==
Despite the extensive grave goods, no human remains were identified in the tomb. This indicates the possibility that it served as a ceremonial site rather than a burial mound, providing valuable insight into the nature of {{ill|satellite tomb|lt=satellite tombs|ja|陪塚}} in the kofun period. Today, the site lies within the grounds of {{ill|Daisen Park|ja|大仙公園}}, where a reconstructed mound of similar size commemorates the original kofun.
== References == {{Reflist}}
== Further Reading == * {{Cite book|ja|author=Sakai City Cultural Affairs Department|title=Heritage of Sakai: The Mozu Kofun Group|publisher=Sakai City|year=2008|pages=15|ref=harv}} * {{Cite book|ja|last=Takada|first=Kanta|chapter=The Significance of Kofun-period Ornaments|title=Seasonal Archaeology No. 106|publisher=Yuzankaku|year=2009|pages=57-61|ref=harv}} * {{Cite book|ja|last=Iida|first=Hiromitsu|chapter=Belt Fittings of Shichikanyama Kofun|title=Autumn Special Exhibit Catalogue: The Tombs of Mozu and Furuichi|publisher=Chikatsu Asuka Museum|year=2011|pages=110-111|ref=harv}} * {{Cite book|ja|author=Sakai City Cultural Affairs Bureau|title=Black Weapons, Silver Armor: The Mozu Kofun Group and 5th-Century Conflicts|series=Sakai City Cultural Heritage Lecture Series Vol. 6|year=2013|ref=harv}} * {{Cite book|ja|author=Shichikan Kofun Research Group|title=Research on Shichikanyama Kofun: Reexamination of the Artifacts Recovered in 1947 and 1952|publisher=Shinyosha|year=2014|ref=harv}}

{{Infobox ancient site |name= Shichikanyama Kofun |image= |alias= Shichikan Kofun |part_of= {{ill|Mozu Kofun Group|ja|百舌鳥古墳群}} |location= {{ill|Sakai-ku|ja|堺区}}, {{ill|Sakai, Osaka|ja|堺市}}, Osaka Prefecture |coordinates= {{coord|34|33|25|N|135|28|43|E|scale:20000}} |type= {{ill|Round Kofun|ja|円墳}} |size= Diameter 56 m |construction_date= [[5th century]] |burial_facility= Wooden Coffin |buried_person= |artifacts= Iron swords, armor, horse gear |designation1= Not designated |designation2= Not designated |notable_features= Destroyed Kofun |map2= {{location map|Japan Osaka|label=Shichikanyama Kofun|lat_deg=34|lat_min=33|lat_sec=25.0|lon_deg=135|lon_min=28|lon_sec=43.0|mark=Round Kofun.svg|marksize=25|float=center|border=none|position=bottom|width=200|caption=location within Osaka Prefecture}} }}
'''Shichikanyama Kofun''' (七観山古墳), also known as '''Shichikan Kofun''' (七観古墳), was a {{ill|Round Kofun|ja|円墳}} located in Asahigaoka Nakamachi 4-chome, {{ill|Sakai-ku|ja|堺区}}, {{ill|Sakai|ja|堺市}}, Osaka Prefecture. Part of the {{ill|Mozu Kofun Group|ja|百舌鳥古墳群}}, the mound was excavated during the Taishō period and again after World War II, yielding a large quantity of iron weapons and armor. It was destroyed in 1952 during land development.
== Overview ==
[[File:480220 Shichikanyama Kofun etc aerial.jpg|thumb|right|250px|Aerial view of Shichikanyama Kofun and surrounding kofun (February 1948). North is at the top. <small>{{National Air Photograph}}</small>]] The kofun was located just northwest of the {{ill|Kamizuka Misanzai Kofun|ja|上石津ミサンザイ古墳}} (attributed to {{ill|Emperor Richū|ja|履中天皇}}), near its northern moat. It was considered a {{ill|satellite tomb|ja|陪塚}} of the Shichikannon Kofun. The mound measured 56 meters in diameter and 8 meters in height, featuring moats, stone paving, and haniwa, including cylindrical haniwa and house-shaped haniwa. Artifacts from the mound suggest it was constructed in two tiers, with a clay burial chamber detected in three locations.
== Excavation and Findings ==
Artifacts recovered during excavations in 1913, 1947, and prior to its destruction in 1952 included: * '''Armor and weapons''': Seven horned helmets (衝角付冑), six or more short-armored cuirasses (短甲), approximately 130 iron swords, serpentine iron swords (蛇行状鉄剣), iron spears, and over 100 iron arrowheads. * '''Horse gear''': Ancient stirrups, regarded as the oldest found in Japan, and gilt bronze fittings for belts, likely imported or influenced by technology from the Korean Peninsula. * '''Other items''': Magatama made from talc, iron axes, chisels, and ceremonial objects.
The bronze belt fittings featured exquisite craftsmanship, including openwork dragon motifs, which closely resemble items found in the {{ill|Imdang-dong Tomb No. 7B|ja|林堂洞7B号墳}} in Gyeongsangbuk-do, Korea. This suggests a connection between the craftsmen or shared cultural influences.
== Historical Significance ==
Despite the extensive grave goods, no human remains were identified in the tomb. This indicates the possibility that it served as a ceremonial site rather than a burial mound, providing valuable insight into the nature of {{ill|satellite tomb|lt=satellite tombs|ja|陪塚}} in the kofun period. Today, the site lies within the grounds of {{ill|Daisen Park|ja|大仙公園}}, where a reconstructed mound of similar size commemorates the original kofun.
== References == {{Reflist}}
== Further Reading == * {{Cite book|ja|author=Sakai City Cultural Affairs Department|title=Heritage of Sakai: The Mozu Kofun Group|publisher=Sakai City|year=2008|pages=15|ref=harv}} * {{Cite book|ja|last=Takada|first=Kanta|chapter=The Significance of Kofun-period Ornaments|title=Seasonal Archaeology No. 106|publisher=Yuzankaku|year=2009|pages=57-61|ref=harv}} * {{Cite book|ja|last=Iida|first=Hiromitsu|chapter=Belt Fittings of Shichikanyama Kofun|title=Autumn Special Exhibit Catalogue: The Tombs of Mozu and Furuichi|publisher=Chikatsu Asuka Museum|year=2011|pages=110-111|ref=harv}} * {{Cite book|ja|author=Sakai City Cultural Affairs Bureau|title=Black Weapons, Silver Armor: The Mozu Kofun Group and 5th-Century Conflicts|series=Sakai City Cultural Heritage Lecture Series Vol. 6|year=2013|ref=harv}} * {{Cite book|ja|author=Shichikan Kofun Research Group|title=Research on Shichikanyama Kofun: Reexamination of the Artifacts Recovered in 1947 and 1952|publisher=Shinyosha|year=2014|ref=harv}}

{{Infobox ancient site |name= Shichikanyama Kofun |image= |alias= Shichikan Kofun |part_of= {{ill|Mozu Kofun Group|ja|百舌鳥古墳群}} |location= {{ill|Sakai-ku|ja|堺区}}, {{ill|Sakai, Osaka|ja|堺市}}, Osaka Prefecture |coordinates= {{coord|34|33|25|N|135|28|43|E|scale:20000}} |type= {{ill|Round Kofun|ja|円墳}} |size= Diameter 56 m |construction_date= [[5th century]] |burial_facility= Wooden Coffin |buried_person= |artifacts= Iron swords, armor, horse gear |designation1= Not designated |designation2= Not designated |notable_features= Destroyed Kofun |map2= {{location map|Japan Osaka|label=Shichikanyama Kofun|lat_deg=34|lat_min=33|lat_sec=25.0|lon_deg=135|lon_min=28|lon_sec=43.0|mark=Round Kofun.svg|marksize=25|float=center|border=none|position=bottom|width=200|caption=location within Osaka Prefecture}} }}
'''Shichikanyama Kofun''' (七観山古墳), also known as '''Shichikan Kofun''' (七観古墳), was a {{ill|Round Kofun|ja|円墳}} located in Asahigaoka Nakamachi 4-chome, {{ill|Sakai-ku|ja|堺区}}, {{ill|Sakai|ja|堺市}}, Osaka Prefecture. Part of the {{ill|Mozu Kofun Group|ja|百舌鳥古墳群}}, the mound was excavated during the Taishō period and again after World War II, yielding a large quantity of iron weapons and armor. It was destroyed in 1952 during land development.
== Overview ==
[[File:480220 Shichikanyama Kofun etc aerial.jpg|thumb|right|250px|Aerial view of Shichikanyama Kofun and surrounding kofun (February 1948). North is at the top. <small>{{National Air Photograph}}</small>]] The kofun was located just northwest of the {{ill|Kamizuka Misanzai Kofun|ja|上石津ミサンザイ古墳}} (attributed to {{ill|Emperor Richū|ja|履中天皇}}), near its northern moat. It was considered a {{ill|satellite tomb|ja|陪塚}} of the Shichikannon Kofun. The mound measured 56 meters in diameter and 8 meters in height, featuring moats, stone paving, and haniwa, including cylindrical haniwa and house-shaped haniwa. Artifacts from the mound suggest it was constructed in two tiers, with a clay burial chamber detected in three locations.
== Excavation and Findings ==
Artifacts recovered during excavations in 1913, 1947, and prior to its destruction in 1952 included: * '''Armor and weapons''': Seven horned helmets (衝角付冑), six or more short-armored cuirasses (短甲), approximately 130 iron swords, serpentine iron swords (蛇行状鉄剣), iron spears, and over 100 iron arrowheads. * '''Horse gear''': Ancient stirrups, regarded as the oldest found in Japan, and gilt bronze fittings for belts, likely imported or influenced by technology from the Korean Peninsula. * '''Other items''': Magatama made from talc, iron axes, chisels, and ceremonial objects.
The bronze belt fittings featured exquisite craftsmanship, including openwork dragon motifs, which closely resemble items found in the {{ill|Imdang-dong Tomb No. 7B|ja|林堂洞7B号墳}} in Gyeongsangbuk-do, Korea. This suggests a connection between the craftsmen or shared cultural influences.
== Historical Significance ==
Despite the extensive grave goods, no human remains were identified in the tomb. This indicates the possibility that it served as a ceremonial site rather than a burial mound, providing valuable insight into the nature of {{ill|satellite tomb|lt=satellite tombs|ja|陪塚}} in the kofun period. Today, the site lies within the grounds of {{ill|Daisen Park|ja|大仙公園}}, where a reconstructed mound of similar size commemorates the original kofun.

{{Infobox ancient site |name= Shichikanyama Kofun |image= |alias= Shichikan Kofun |part_of= {{ill|Mozu Kofun Group|ja|百舌鳥古墳群}} |location= {{ill|Sakai-ku|ja|堺区}}, {{ill|Sakai, Osaka|ja|堺市}}, Osaka Prefecture |coordinates= {{coord|34|33|25|N|135|28|43|E|scale:20000}} |type= {{ill|Round Kofun|ja|円墳}} |size= Diameter 56 m |construction_date= [[5th century]] |burial_facility= Wooden Coffin |buried_person= |artifacts= Iron swords, armor, horse gear |designation1= Not designated |designation2= Not designated |notable_features= Destroyed Kofun |map2= {{location map|Japan Osaka|label=Shichikanyama Kofun|lat_deg=34|lat_min=33|lat_sec=25.0|lon_deg=135|lon_min=28|lon_sec=43.0|mark=Round Kofun.svg|marksize=25|float=center|border=none|position=bottom|width=200|caption=location within Osaka Prefecture}} }}
'''Shichikanyama Kofun''' (七観山古墳), also known as '''Shichikan Kofun''' (七観古墳), was a {{ill|Round Kofun|ja|円墳}} located in Asahigaoka Nakamachi 4-chome, {{ill|Sakai-ku|ja|堺区}}, {{ill|Sakai|ja|堺市}}, Osaka Prefecture. Part of the {{ill|Mozu Kofun Group|ja|百舌鳥古墳群}}, the mound was excavated during the Taishō period and again after World War II, yielding a large quantity of iron weapons and armor. It was destroyed in 1952 during land development.
== Overview ==
[[File:480220 Shichikanyama Kofun etc aerial.jpg|thumb|right|250px|Aerial view of Shichikanyama Kofun and surrounding kofun (February 1948). North is at the top. <small>{{National Air Photograph}}</small>]] The kofun was located just northwest of the {{ill|Kamizuka Misanzai Kofun|ja|上石津ミサンザイ古墳}} (attributed to {{ill|Emperor Richū|ja|履中天皇}}), near its northern moat. It was considered a {{ill|satellite tomb|ja|陪塚}} of the Shichikannon Kofun. The mound measured 56 meters in diameter and 8 meters in height, featuring moats, stone paving, and haniwa, including cylindrical haniwa and house-shaped haniwa. Artifacts from the mound suggest it was constructed in two tiers, with a clay burial chamber detected in three locations.
== Excavation and Findings ==
Artifacts recovered during excavations in 1913, 1947, and prior to its destruction in 1952 included: * '''Armor and weapons''': Seven horned helmets (衝角付冑), six or more short-armored cuirasses (短甲), approximately 130 iron swords, serpentine iron swords (蛇行状鉄剣), iron spears, and over 100 iron arrowheads. * '''Horse gear''': Ancient stirrups, regarded as the oldest found in Japan, and gilt bronze fittings for belts, likely imported or influenced by technology from the Korean Peninsula. * '''Other items''': Magatama made from talc, iron axes, chisels, and ceremonial objects.
The bronze belt fittings featured exquisite craftsmanship, including openwork dragon motifs, which closely resemble items found in the {{ill|Imdang-dong Tomb No. 7B|ja|林堂洞7B号墳}} in Gyeongsangbuk-do, Korea. This suggests a connection between the craftsmen or shared cultural influences.
== Historical Significance ==
Despite the extensive grave goods, no human remains were identified in the tomb. This indicates the possibility that it served as a ceremonial site rather than a burial mound, providing valuable insight into the nature of {{ill|satellite tomb|lt=satellite tombs|ja|陪塚}} in the kofun period. Today, the site lies within the grounds of {{ill|Daisen Park|ja|大仙公園}}, where a reconstructed mound of similar size commemorates the original kofun.
== References == {{Reflist}}
== Further Reading == * {{Cite book|ja|author=Sakai City Cultural Affairs Department|title=Heritage of Sakai: The Mozu Kofun Group|publisher=Sakai City|year=2008|pages=15|ref=harv}} * {{Cite book|ja|last=Takada|first=Kanta|chapter=The Significance of Kofun-period Ornaments|title=Seasonal Archaeology No. 106|publisher=Yuzankaku|year=2009|pages=57-61|ref=harv}} * {{Cite book|ja|last=Iida|first=Hiromitsu|chapter=Belt Fittings of Shichikanyama Kofun|title=Autumn Special Exhibit Catalogue: The Tombs of Mozu and Furuichi|publisher=Chikatsu Asuka Museum|year=2011|pages=110-111|ref=harv}} * {{Cite book|ja|author=Sakai City Cultural Affairs Bureau|title=Black Weapons, Silver Armor: The Mozu Kofun Group and 5th-Century Conflicts|series=Sakai City Cultural Heritage Lecture Series Vol. 6|year=2013|ref=harv}} * {{Cite book|ja|author=Shichikan Kofun Research Group|title=Research on Shichikanyama Kofun: Reexamination of the Artifacts Recovered in 1947 and 1952|publisher=Shinyosha|year=2014|ref=harv}}

'''Shichikanyama Kofun''' (七観山古墳), also known as '''Shichikan Kofun''' (七観古墳), was a {{ill|Round Kofun|ja|円墳}} located in Asahigaoka Nakamachi 4-chome, {{ill|Sakai-ku|ja|堺区}}, {{ill|Sakai|ja|堺市}}, Osaka Prefecture. Part of the {{ill|Mozu Kofun Group|ja|百舌鳥古墳群}}, the mound was excavated during the Taishō period and again after World War II, yielding a large quantity of iron weapons and armor. It was destroyed in 1952 during land development.
== Overview ==
[[File:480220 Shichikanyama Kofun etc aerial.jpg|thumb|right|250px|Aerial view of Shichikanyama Kofun and surrounding kofun (February 1948). North is at the top. <small>{{National Air Photograph}}</small>]] The kofun was located just northwest of the {{ill|Kamizuka Misanzai Kofun|ja|上石津ミサンザイ古墳}} (attributed to {{ill|Emperor Richū|ja|履中天皇}}), near its northern moat. It was considered a {{ill|satellite tomb|ja|陪塚}} of the Shichikannon Kofun. The mound measured 56 meters in diameter and 8 meters in height, featuring moats, stone paving, and haniwa, including cylindrical haniwa and house-shaped haniwa. Artifacts from the mound suggest it was constructed in two tiers, with a clay burial chamber detected in three locations.

Artifacts recovered during excavations in 1913, 1947, and prior to its destruction in 1952 included: * '''Armor and weapons''': Seven horned helmets (衝角付冑), six or more short-armored cuirasses (短甲), approximately 130 iron swords, serpentine iron swords (蛇行状鉄剣), iron spears, and over 100 iron arrowheads. * '''Horse gear''': Ancient stirrups, regarded as the oldest found in Japan, and gilt bronze fittings for belts, likely imported or influenced by technology from the Korean Peninsula. * '''Other items''': Magatama made from talc, iron axes, chisels, and ceremonial objects.

== Historical Significance ==
Despite the extensive grave goods, no human remains were identified in the tomb. This indicates the possibility that it served as a ceremonial site rather than a burial mound, providing valuable insight into the nature of {{ill|satellite tomb|lt=satellite tombs|ja|陪塚}} in the kofun period. Today, the site lies within the grounds of {{ill|Daisen Park|ja|大仙公園}}, where a reconstructed mound of similar size commemorates the original kofun.

{{Infobox ancient site |name= Shichikanyama Kofun |image= |alias= Shichikan Kofun |part_of= {{ill|Mozu Kofun Group|ja|百舌鳥古墳群}} |location= {{ill|Sakai-ku|ja|堺区}}, {{ill|Sakai, Osaka|ja|堺市}}, Osaka Prefecture |coordinates= {{coord|34|33|25|N|135|28|43|E|scale:20000}} |type= {{ill|Round Kofun|ja|円墳}} |size= Diameter 56 m |construction_date= [[5th century]] |burial_facility= Wooden Coffin |buried_person= |artifacts= Iron swords, armor, horse gear |designation1= Not designated |designation2= Not designated |notable_features= Destroyed Kofun |map2= {{location map|Japan Osaka|label=Shichikanyama Kofun|lat_deg=34|lat_min=33|lat_sec=25.0|lon_deg=135|lon_min=28|lon_sec=43.0|mark=Round Kofun.svg|marksize=25|float=center|border=none|position=bottom|width=200|caption=location within Osaka Prefecture}} }}
'''Shichikanyama Kofun''' (七観山古墳), also known as '''Shichikan Kofun''' (七観古墳), was a {{ill|Round Kofun|ja|円墳}} located in Asahigaoka Nakamachi 4-chome, {{ill|Sakai-ku|ja|堺区}}, {{ill|Sakai|ja|堺市}}, Osaka Prefecture. Part of the {{ill|Mozu Kofun Group|ja|百舌鳥古墳群}}, the mound was excavated during the Taishō period and again after World War II, yielding a large quantity of iron weapons and armor. It was destroyed in 1952 during land development.
== Overview ==
[[File:480220 Shichikanyama Kofun etc aerial.jpg|thumb|right|250px|Aerial view of Shichikanyama Kofun and surrounding kofun (February 1948). North is at the top. <small>{{National Air Photograph}}</small>]] The kofun was located just northwest of the {{ill|Kamizuka Misanzai Kofun|ja|上石津ミサンザイ古墳}} (attributed to {{ill|Emperor Richū|ja|履中天皇}}), near its northern moat. It was considered a {{ill|satellite tomb|ja|陪塚}} of the Shichikannon Kofun. The mound measured 56 meters in diameter and 8 meters in height, featuring moats, stone paving, and haniwa, including cylindrical haniwa and house-shaped haniwa. Artifacts from the mound suggest it was constructed in two tiers, with a clay burial chamber detected in three locations.
== Excavation and Findings ==
Artifacts recovered during excavations in 1913, 1947, and prior to its destruction in 1952 included: * '''Armor and weapons''': Seven horned helmets (衝角付冑), six or more short-armored cuirasses (短甲), approximately 130 iron swords, serpentine iron swords (蛇行状鉄剣), iron spears, and over 100 iron arrowheads. * '''Horse gear''': Ancient stirrups, regarded as the oldest found in Japan, and gilt bronze fittings for belts, likely imported or influenced by technology from the Korean Peninsula. * '''Other items''': Magatama made from talc, iron axes, chisels, and ceremonial objects.
The bronze belt fittings featured exquisite craftsmanship, including openwork dragon motifs, which closely resemble items found in the {{ill|Imdang-dong Tomb No. 7B|ja|林堂洞7B号墳}} in Gyeongsangbuk-do, Korea. This suggests a connection between the craftsmen or shared cultural influences.
== Historical Significance ==
Despite the extensive grave goods, no human remains were identified in the tomb. This indicates the possibility that it served as a ceremonial site rather than a burial mound, providing valuable insight into the nature of {{ill|satellite tomb|lt=satellite tombs|ja|陪塚}} in the kofun period. Today, the site lies within the grounds of {{ill|Daisen Park|ja|大仙公園}}, where a reconstructed mound of similar size commemorates the original kofun.
== References == {{Reflist}}
== Further Reading == * {{Cite book|ja|author=Sakai City Cultural Affairs Department|title=Heritage of Sakai: The Mozu Kofun Group|publisher=Sakai City|year=2008|pages=15|ref=harv}} * {{Cite book|ja|last=Takada|first=Kanta|chapter=The Significance of Kofun-period Ornaments|title=Seasonal Archaeology No. 106|publisher=Yuzankaku|year=2009|pages=57-61|ref=harv}} * {{Cite book|ja|last=Iida|first=Hiromitsu|chapter=Belt Fittings of Shichikanyama Kofun|title=Autumn Special Exhibit Catalogue: The Tombs of Mozu and Furuichi|publisher=Chikatsu Asuka Museum|year=2011|pages=110-111|ref=harv}} * {{Cite book|ja|author=Sakai City Cultural Affairs Bureau|title=Black Weapons, Silver Armor: The Mozu Kofun Group and 5th-Century Conflicts|series=Sakai City Cultural Heritage Lecture Series Vol. 6|year=2013|ref=harv}} * {{Cite book|ja|author=Shichikan Kofun Research Group|title=Research on Shichikanyama Kofun: Reexamination of the Artifacts Recovered in 1947 and 1952|publisher=Shinyosha|year=2014|ref=harv}}
