revision.comment: Copying from [[Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] to [[Category:qq]][[sn:{{subst:PAGENAME}}]][[Category:qq]] using [[c:Help:Cat-a-lot|Cat-a-lot]]
revision.text:
{{afc comment|1=trimming these off. the parameter works as "burial" so no need to use "Burial Facility"[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 03:35, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=added parameters Interred_individual and Mausoleum is planned to be added but I messed up in template editing[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:40, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=now we have a 'burial' and 'burial goods' parameter. So a lot of these pages might not be marked on our wiki as having unknown parameters. But specifically keeping this comment here in case of import, these parameters should be added to [[Template:infobox ancient site]] rather than deleted from this draft or article[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:02, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under [[:Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones[[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = {{ill|Round Kofun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun}} |name= Shichikanyama Kofun |image= |alternate_name= Shichikan Kofun | part_of= {{ill|Mozu Kofun Group|ja|百舌鳥古墳群|1=Mozu Tombs}} |location= {{ill|Sakai-ku|ja|堺区}}, {{ill|Sakai, Osaka|ja|堺市}}, Osaka Prefecture |coordinates= {{coord|34|33|25|N|135|28|43|E|map_size:20000}} |type= {{ill|Round Kofun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun}} |size= Diameter 56 m |built= [[5th century]] |burial= Wooden Coffin |buried_person= |burial goods= Iron swords, armor, horse gear |other_designation1= Not other_designation1 |other_designation2= Not other_designation1 |notable_features= Destroyed Kofun |map= {{location map|Japan Osaka|map_dot_label=Shichikanyama Kofun|lat_deg=34|lat_min=33|lat_sec=25.0|lon_deg=135|lon_min=28|lon_sec=43.0|mark=Round Kofun.svg|marksize=25|float=center|border=none|position=bottom|width=200|caption=location within Osaka Prefecture}} }}
'''Shichikanyama Kofun''' (七観山古墳), also known as '''Shichikan Kofun''' (七観古墳), was a {{ill|Round Kofun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun}} located in Asahigaoka Nakamachi 4-chome, {{ill|Sakai-ku|ja|堺区}}, {{ill|Sakai|ja|堺市}}, Osaka Prefecture. Part of the {{ill|Mozu Kofun Group|ja|百舌鳥古墳群|1=Mozu Tombs}}, the mound was excavated during the Taishō period and again after World War II, yielding a large quantity of iron weapons and armor. It was destroyed in 1952 during land development.
== Overview ==
[[File:480220 Shichikanyama Kofun etc aerial.jpg|thumb|right|250px|Aerial view of Shichikanyama Kofun and surrounding kofun (February 1948). North is at the top. <small>{{National Air Photograph}}</small>]] The kofun was located just northwest of the {{ill|Kamizuka Misanzai Kofun|ja|上石津ミサンザイ古墳|1=Kami-Ishizu Misanzai Kofun}} (attributed to {{ill|Emperor Richū|ja|履中天皇}}), near its northern moat. It was considered a {{ill|satellite tomb|ja|陪塚}} of the Shichikannon Kofun. The mound measured 56 meters in diameter and 8 meters in height, featuring moats, stone paving, and haniwa, including cylindrical haniwa and house-shaped haniwa. burial goods from the mound suggest it was constructed in two tiers, with a clay burial chamber detected in three locations.
== Excavation and Findings ==
burial goods recovered during excavations in 1913, 1947, and prior to its destruction in 1952 included: * '''Armor and weapons''': Seven horned helmets (衝角付冑), six or more short-armored cuirasses (短甲), approximately 130 iron swords, serpentine iron swords (蛇行状鉄剣), iron spears, and over 100 iron arrowheads. * '''Horse gear''': Ancient stirrups, regarded as the oldest found in Japan, and gilt bronze fittings for belts, likely imported or influenced by technology from the Korean Peninsula. * '''Other items''': Magatama made from talc, iron axes, chisels, and ceremonial objects.
The bronze belt fittings featured exquisite craftsmanship, including openwork dragon motifs, which closely resemble items found in the {{ill|Imdang-dong Tomb No. 7B|ja|林堂洞7B号墳}} in Gyeongsangbuk-do, Korea. This suggests a connection between the craftsmen or shared cultural influences.
== Historical Significance ==
Despite the extensive grave goods, no human remains were identified in the tomb. This indicates the possibility that it served as a ceremonial site rather than a Kofun, providing valuable insight into the nature of {{ill|satellite tomb|lt=satellite tombs|ja|陪塚}} in the kofun period. Today, the site lies within the grounds of {{ill|Daisen Park|ja|大仙公園}}, where a reconstructed mound of similar size commemorates the original kofun.
== References == {{Reflist}}
== Further Reading == * {{Cite book|ja|author=Sakai City Cultural Affairs Department|title=Heritage of Sakai: The Mozu Kofun Group|publisher=Sakai City|year=2008|pages=15|ref=harv}} * {{Cite book|ja|last=Takada|first=Kanta|chapter=The Significance of Kofun-period Ornaments|title=Seasonal Archaeology No. 106|publisher=Yuzankaku|year=2009|pages=57-61|ref=harv}} * {{Cite book|ja|last=Iida|first=Hiromitsu|chapter=Belt Fittings of Shichikanyama Kofun|title=Autumn Special Exhibit Catalogue: The Tombs of Mozu and Furuichi|publisher=Chikatsu Asuka Museum|year=2011|pages=110-111|ref=harv}} * {{Cite book|ja|author=Sakai City Cultural Affairs Bureau|title=Black Weapons, Silver Armor: The Mozu Kofun Group and 5th-Century Conflicts|series=Sakai City other_other_designation Lecture Series Vol. 6|year=2013|ref=harv}} * {{Cite book|ja|author=Shichikan Kofun Research Group|title=Research on Shichikanyama Kofun: Reexamination of the burial goods Recovered in 1947 and 1952|publisher=Shinyosha|year=2014|ref=harv}}
== Related Articles == * {{ill|Mozu Kofun Group|ja|百舌鳥古墳群|1=Mozu Tombs}} * {{ill|Nochi Kofun|ja|野中古墳}}
== External Links == * [https://www.city.sakai.lg.jp/kanko/rekishi/dkofun/database/shichikanyama.html Digital Kofun Encyclopedia: Shichikanyama Kofun] (Sakai City Official Website)

Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = {{ill|Round Kofun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun}} |name= Shichikanyama Kofun |image= |alternate_name= Shichikan Kofun | part_of= {{ill|Mozu Kofun Group|ja|百舌鳥古墳群|1=Mozu Tombs}} |location= {{ill|Sakai-ku|ja|堺区}}, {{ill|Sakai, Osaka|ja|堺市}}, Osaka Prefecture |coordinates= {{coord|34|33|25|N|135|28|43|E|map_size:20000}} |type= {{ill|Round Kofun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun}} |size= Diameter 56 m |built= [[5th century]] |burial= Wooden Coffin |buried_person= |burial goods= Iron swords, armor, horse gear |other_designation1= Not other_designation1 |other_designation2= Not other_designation1 |notable_features= Destroyed Kofun |map= {{location map|Japan Osaka|map_dot_label=Shichikanyama Kofun|lat_deg=34|lat_min=33|lat_sec=25.0|lon_deg=135|lon_min=28|lon_sec=43.0|mark=Round Kofun.svg|marksize=25|float=center|border=none|position=bottom|width=200|caption=location within Osaka Prefecture}} }}
'''Shichikanyama Kofun''' (七観山古墳), also known as '''Shichikan Kofun''' (七観古墳), was a {{ill|Round Kofun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun}} located in Asahigaoka Nakamachi 4-chome, {{ill|Sakai-ku|ja|堺区}}, {{ill|Sakai|ja|堺市}}, Osaka Prefecture. Part of the {{ill|Mozu Kofun Group|ja|百舌鳥古墳群|1=Mozu Tombs}}, the mound was excavated during the Taishō period and again after World War II, yielding a large quantity of iron weapons and armor. It was destroyed in 1952 during land development.
== Overview ==
[[File:480220 Shichikanyama Kofun etc aerial.jpg|thumb|right|250px|Aerial view of Shichikanyama Kofun and surrounding kofun (February 1948). North is at the top. <small>{{National Air Photograph}}</small>]] The kofun was located just northwest of the {{ill|Kamizuka Misanzai Kofun|ja|上石津ミサンザイ古墳|1=Kami-Ishizu Misanzai Kofun}} (attributed to {{ill|Emperor Richū|ja|履中天皇}}), near its northern moat. It was considered a {{ill|satellite tomb|ja|陪塚}} of the Shichikannon Kofun. The mound measured 56 meters in diameter and 8 meters in height, featuring moats, stone paving, and haniwa, including cylindrical haniwa and house-shaped haniwa. burial goods from the mound suggest it was constructed in two tiers, with a clay burial chamber detected in three locations.
== Excavation and Findings ==
burial goods recovered during excavations in 1913, 1947, and prior to its destruction in 1952 included: * '''Armor and weapons''': Seven horned helmets (衝角付冑), six or more short-armored cuirasses (短甲), approximately 130 iron swords, serpentine iron swords (蛇行状鉄剣), iron spears, and over 100 iron arrowheads. * '''Horse gear''': Ancient stirrups, regarded as the oldest found in Japan, and gilt bronze fittings for belts, likely imported or influenced by technology from the Korean Peninsula. * '''Other items''': Magatama made from talc, iron axes, chisels, and ceremonial objects.
The bronze belt fittings featured exquisite craftsmanship, including openwork dragon motifs, which closely resemble items found in the {{ill|Imdang-dong Tomb No. 7B|ja|林堂洞7B号墳}} in Gyeongsangbuk-do, Korea. This suggests a connection between the craftsmen or shared cultural influences.
== Historical Significance ==
Despite the extensive grave goods, no human remains were identified in the tomb. This indicates the possibility that it served as a ceremonial site rather than a Kofun, providing valuable insight into the nature of {{ill|satellite tomb|lt=satellite tombs|ja|陪塚}} in the kofun period. Today, the site lies within the grounds of {{ill|Daisen Park|ja|大仙公園}}, where a reconstructed mound of similar size commemorates the original kofun.
== References == {{Reflist}}
== Further Reading == * {{Cite book|ja|author=Sakai City Cultural Affairs Department|title=Heritage of Sakai: The Mozu Kofun Group|publisher=Sakai City|year=2008|pages=15|ref=harv}} * {{Cite book|ja|last=Takada|first=Kanta|chapter=The Significance of Kofun-period Ornaments|title=Seasonal Archaeology No. 106|publisher=Yuzankaku|year=2009|pages=57-61|ref=harv}} * {{Cite book|ja|last=Iida|first=Hiromitsu|chapter=Belt Fittings of Shichikanyama Kofun|title=Autumn Special Exhibit Catalogue: The Tombs of Mozu and Furuichi|publisher=Chikatsu Asuka Museum|year=2011|pages=110-111|ref=harv}} * {{Cite book|ja|author=Sakai City Cultural Affairs Bureau|title=Black Weapons, Silver Armor: The Mozu Kofun Group and 5th-Century Conflicts|series=Sakai City other_designation Lecture Series Vol. 6|year=2013|ref=harv}} * {{Cite book|ja|author=Shichikan Kofun Research Group|title=Research on Shichikanyama Kofun: Reexamination of the burial goods Recovered in 1947 and 1952|publisher=Shinyosha|year=2014|ref=harv}}
== Related Articles == * {{ill|Mozu Kofun Group|ja|百舌鳥古墳群|1=Mozu Tombs}} * {{ill|Nochi Kofun|ja|野中古墳}}
== External Links == * [https://www.city.sakai.lg.jp/kanko/rekishi/dkofun/database/shichikanyama.html Digital Kofun Encyclopedia: Shichikanyama Kofun] (Sakai City Official Website)

burial goods recovered during excavations in 1913, 1947, and prior to its destruction in 1952 included: * '''Armor and weapons''': Seven horned helmets (衝角付冑), six or more short-armored cuirasses (短甲), approximately 130 iron swords, serpentine iron swords (蛇行状鉄剣), iron spears, and over 100 iron arrowheads. * '''Horse gear''': Ancient stirrups, regarded as the oldest found in Japan, and gilt bronze fittings for belts, likely imported or influenced by technology from the Korean Peninsula. * '''Other items''': Magatama made from talc, iron axes, chisels, and ceremonial objects.
The bronze belt fittings featured exquisite craftsmanship, including openwork dragon motifs, which closely resemble items found in the {{ill|Imdang-dong Tomb No. 7B|ja|林堂洞7B号墳}} in Gyeongsangbuk-do, Korea. This suggests a connection between the craftsmen or shared cultural influences.
== Historical Significance ==
Despite the extensive grave goods, no human remains were identified in the tomb. This indicates the possibility that it served as a ceremonial site rather than a Kofun, providing valuable insight into the nature of {{ill|satellite tomb|lt=satellite tombs|ja|陪塚}} in the kofun period. Today, the site lies within the grounds of {{ill|Daisen Park|ja|大仙公園}}, where a reconstructed mound of similar size commemorates the original kofun.

'''Shichikanyama Kofun''' (七観山古墳), also known as '''Shichikan Kofun''' (七観古墳), was a {{ill|Round Kofun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun}} located in Asahigaoka Nakamachi 4-chome, {{ill|Sakai-ku|ja|堺区}}, {{ill|Sakai|ja|堺市}}, Osaka Prefecture. Part of the {{ill|Mozu Kofun Group|ja|百舌鳥古墳群|1=Mozu Tombs}}, the mound was excavated during the Taishō period and again after World War II, yielding a large quantity of iron weapons and armor. It was destroyed in 1952 during land development.
== Overview ==
[[File:480220 Shichikanyama Kofun etc aerial.jpg|thumb|right|250px|Aerial view of Shichikanyama Kofun and surrounding kofun (February 1948). North is at the top. <small>{{National Air Photograph}}</small>]] The kofun was located just northwest of the {{ill|Kamizuka Misanzai Kofun|ja|上石津ミサンザイ古墳|1=Kami-Ishizu Misanzai Kofun}} (attributed to {{ill|Emperor Richū|ja|履中天皇}}), near its northern moat. It was considered a {{ill|satellite tomb|ja|陪塚}} of the Shichikannon Kofun. The mound measured 56 meters in diameter and 8 meters in height, featuring moats, stone paving, and haniwa, including cylindrical haniwa and house-shaped haniwa. burial goods from the mound suggest it was constructed in two tiers, with a clay burial chamber detected in three locations.
== Excavation and Findings ==
burial goods recovered during excavations in 1913, 1947, and prior to its destruction in 1952 included: * '''Armor and weapons''': Seven horned helmets (衝角付冑), six or more short-armored cuirasses (短甲), approximately 130 iron swords, serpentine iron swords (蛇行状鉄剣), iron spears, and over 100 iron arrowheads. * '''Horse gear''': Ancient stirrups, regarded as the oldest found in Japan, and gilt bronze fittings for belts, likely imported or influenced by technology from the Korean Peninsula. * '''Other items''': Magatama made from talc, iron axes, chisels, and ceremonial objects.
The bronze belt fittings featured exquisite craftsmanship, including openwork dragon motifs, which closely resemble items found in the {{ill|Imdang-dong Tomb No. 7B|ja|林堂洞7B号墳}} in Gyeongsangbuk-do, Korea. This suggests a connection between the craftsmen or shared cultural influences.
== Historical Significance ==
Despite the extensive grave goods, no human remains were identified in the tomb. This indicates the possibility that it served as a ceremonial site rather than a Kofun, providing valuable insight into the nature of {{ill|satellite tomb|lt=satellite tombs|ja|陪塚}} in the kofun period. Today, the site lies within the grounds of {{ill|Daisen Park|ja|大仙公園}}, where a reconstructed mound of similar size commemorates the original kofun.
== References == {{Reflist}}
== Further Reading == * {{Cite book|ja|author=Sakai City Cultural Affairs Department|title=Heritage of Sakai: The Mozu Kofun Group|publisher=Sakai City|year=2008|pages=15|ref=harv}} * {{Cite book|ja|last=Takada|first=Kanta|chapter=The Significance of Kofun-period Ornaments|title=Seasonal Archaeology No. 106|publisher=Yuzankaku|year=2009|pages=57-61|ref=harv}} * {{Cite book|ja|last=Iida|first=Hiromitsu|chapter=Belt Fittings of Shichikanyama Kofun|title=Autumn Special Exhibit Catalogue: The Tombs of Mozu and Furuichi|publisher=Chikatsu Asuka Museum|year=2011|pages=110-111|ref=harv}} * {{Cite book|ja|author=Sakai City Cultural Affairs Bureau|title=Black Weapons, Silver Armor: The Mozu Kofun Group and 5th-Century Conflicts|series=Sakai City other_designation Lecture Series Vol. 6|year=2013|ref=harv}} * {{Cite book|ja|author=Shichikan Kofun Research Group|title=Research on Shichikanyama Kofun: Reexamination of the burial goods Recovered in 1947 and 1952|publisher=Shinyosha|year=2014|ref=harv}}

'''Shichikanyama Kofun''' (七観山古墳), also known as '''Shichikan Kofun''' (七観古墳), was a {{ill|Round Kofun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun}} located in Asahigaoka Nakamachi 4-chome, {{ill|Sakai-ku|ja|堺区}}, {{ill|Sakai|ja|堺市}}, Osaka Prefecture. Part of the {{ill|Mozu Kofun Group|ja|百舌鳥古墳群|1=Mozu Tombs}}, the mound was excavated during the Taishō period and again after World War II, yielding a large quantity of iron weapons and armor. It was destroyed in 1952 during land development.
== Overview ==
[[File:480220 Shichikanyama Kofun etc aerial.jpg|thumb|right|250px|Aerial view of Shichikanyama Kofun and surrounding kofun (February 1948). North is at the top. <small>{{National Air Photograph}}</small>]] The kofun was located just northwest of the {{ill|Kamizuka Misanzai Kofun|ja|上石津ミサンザイ古墳|1=Kami-Ishizu Misanzai Kofun}} (attributed to {{ill|Emperor Richū|ja|履中天皇}}), near its northern moat. It was considered a {{ill|satellite tomb|ja|陪塚}} of the Shichikannon Kofun. The mound measured 56 meters in diameter and 8 meters in height, featuring moats, stone paving, and haniwa, including cylindrical haniwa and house-shaped haniwa. burial goods from the mound suggest it was constructed in two tiers, with a clay burial chamber detected in three locations.
== Excavation and Findings ==
burial goods recovered during excavations in 1913, 1947, and prior to its destruction in 1952 included: * '''Armor and weapons''': Seven horned helmets (衝角付冑), six or more short-armored cuirasses (短甲), approximately 130 iron swords, serpentine iron swords (蛇行状鉄剣), iron spears, and over 100 iron arrowheads. * '''Horse gear''': Ancient stirrups, regarded as the oldest found in Japan, and gilt bronze fittings for belts, likely imported or influenced by technology from the Korean Peninsula. * '''Other items''': Magatama made from talc, iron axes, chisels, and ceremonial objects.
The bronze belt fittings featured exquisite craftsmanship, including openwork dragon motifs, which closely resemble items found in the {{ill|Imdang-dong Tomb No. 7B|ja|林堂洞7B号墳}} in Gyeongsangbuk-do, Korea. This suggests a connection between the craftsmen or shared cultural influences.
== Historical Significance ==
Despite the extensive grave goods, no human remains were identified in the tomb. This indicates the possibility that it served as a ceremonial site rather than a Kofun, providing valuable insight into the nature of {{ill|satellite tomb|lt=satellite tombs|ja|陪塚}} in the kofun period. Today, the site lies within the grounds of {{ill|Daisen Park|ja|大仙公園}}, where a reconstructed mound of similar size commemorates the original kofun.

'''Shichikanyama Kofun''' (七観山古墳), also known as '''Shichikan Kofun''' (七観古墳), was a {{ill|Round Kofun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun}} located in Asahigaoka Nakamachi 4-chome, {{ill|Sakai-ku|ja|堺区}}, {{ill|Sakai|ja|堺市}}, Osaka Prefecture. Part of the {{ill|Mozu Kofun Group|ja|百舌鳥古墳群|1=Mozu Tombs}}, the mound was excavated during the Taishō period and again after World War II, yielding a large quantity of iron weapons and armor. It was destroyed in 1952 during land development.
== Overview ==
[[File:480220 Shichikanyama Kofun etc aerial.jpg|thumb|right|250px|Aerial view of Shichikanyama Kofun and surrounding kofun (February 1948). North is at the top. <small>{{National Air Photograph}}</small>]] The kofun was located just northwest of the {{ill|Kamizuka Misanzai Kofun|ja|上石津ミサンザイ古墳|1=Kami-Ishizu Misanzai Kofun}} (attributed to {{ill|Emperor Richū|ja|履中天皇}}), near its northern moat. It was considered a {{ill|satellite tomb|ja|陪塚}} of the Shichikannon Kofun. The mound measured 56 meters in diameter and 8 meters in height, featuring moats, stone paving, and haniwa, including cylindrical haniwa and house-shaped haniwa. burial goods from the mound suggest it was constructed in two tiers, with a clay burial chamber detected in three locations.
== Excavation and Findings ==
burial goods recovered during excavations in 1913, 1947, and prior to its destruction in 1952 included: * '''Armor and weapons''': Seven horned helmets (衝角付冑), six or more short-armored cuirasses (短甲), approximately 130 iron swords, serpentine iron swords (蛇行状鉄剣), iron spears, and over 100 iron arrowheads. * '''Horse gear''': Ancient stirrups, regarded as the oldest found in Japan, and gilt bronze fittings for belts, likely imported or influenced by technology from the Korean Peninsula. * '''Other items''': Magatama made from talc, iron axes, chisels, and ceremonial objects.
The bronze belt fittings featured exquisite craftsmanship, including openwork dragon motifs, which closely resemble items found in the {{ill|Imdang-dong Tomb No. 7B|ja|林堂洞7B号墳}} in Gyeongsangbuk-do, Korea. This suggests a connection between the craftsmen or shared cultural influences.
== Historical Significance ==
Despite the extensive grave goods, no human remains were identified in the tomb. This indicates the possibility that it served as a ceremonial site rather than a Kofun, providing valuable insight into the nature of {{ill|satellite tomb|lt=satellite tombs|ja|陪塚}} in the kofun period. Today, the site lies within the grounds of {{ill|Daisen Park|ja|大仙公園}}, where a reconstructed mound of similar size commemorates the original kofun.

Today, the site lies within the grounds of {{ill|Daisen Park|ja|大仙公園}}, where a reconstructed mound of similar size commemorates the original kofun.

'''Shichikanyama Kofun''' (七観山古墳), also known as '''Shichikan Kofun''' (七観古墳), was a {{ill|Round Kofun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun}} located in Asahigaoka Nakamachi 4-chome, {{ill|Sakai-ku|ja|堺区}}, {{ill|Sakai|ja|堺市}}, Osaka Prefecture. Part of the {{ill|Mozu Kofun Group|ja|百舌鳥古墳群|1=Mozu Tombs}}, the mound was excavated during the Taishō period and again after World War II, yielding a large quantity of iron weapons and armor. It was destroyed in 1952 during land development.
== Overview ==
[[File:480220 Shichikanyama Kofun etc aerial.jpg|thumb|right|250px|Aerial view of Shichikanyama Kofun and surrounding kofun (February 1948). North is at the top. <small>{{National Air Photograph}}</small>]] The kofun was located just northwest of the {{ill|Kamizuka Misanzai Kofun|ja|上石津ミサンザイ古墳|1=Kami-Ishizu Misanzai Kofun}} (attributed to {{ill|Emperor Richū|ja|履中天皇}}), near its northern moat. It was considered a {{ill|satellite tomb|ja|陪塚}} of the Shichikannon Kofun. The mound measured 56 meters in diameter and 8 meters in height, featuring moats, stone paving, and haniwa, including cylindrical haniwa and house-shaped haniwa. burial goods from the mound suggest it was constructed in two tiers, with a clay burial chamber detected in three locations.
== Excavation and Findings ==
burial goods recovered during excavations in 1913, 1947, and prior to its destruction in 1952 included: * '''Armor and weapons''': Seven horned helmets (衝角付冑), six or more short-armored cuirasses (短甲), approximately 130 iron swords, serpentine iron swords (蛇行状鉄剣), iron spears, and over 100 iron arrowheads. * '''Horse gear''': Ancient stirrups, regarded as the oldest found in Japan, and gilt bronze fittings for belts, likely imported or influenced by technology from the Korean Peninsula. * '''Other items''': Magatama made from talc, iron axes, chisels, and ceremonial objects.
The bronze belt fittings featured exquisite craftsmanship, including openwork dragon motifs, which closely resemble items found in the {{ill|Imdang-dong Tomb No. 7B|ja|林堂洞7B号墳}} in Gyeongsangbuk-do, Korea. This suggests a connection between the craftsmen or shared cultural influences.
== Historical Significance ==
Despite the extensive grave goods, no human remains were identified in the tomb. This indicates the possibility that it served as a ceremonial site rather than a Kofun, providing valuable insight into the nature of {{ill|satellite tomb|lt=satellite tombs|ja|陪塚}} in the kofun period. Today, the site lies within the grounds of {{ill|Daisen Park|ja|大仙公園}}, where a reconstructed mound of similar size commemorates the original kofun.

'''Shichikanyama Kofun''' (七観山古墳), also known as '''Shichikan Kofun''' (七観古墳), was a {{ill|Round Kofun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun}} located in Asahigaoka Nakamachi 4-chome, {{ill|Sakai-ku|ja|堺区}}, {{ill|Sakai|ja|堺市}}, Osaka Prefecture. Part of the {{ill|Mozu Kofun Group|ja|百舌鳥古墳群|1=Mozu Tombs}}, the mound was excavated during the Taishō period and again after World War II, yielding a large quantity of iron weapons and armor. It was destroyed in 1952 during land development.
== Overview ==
[[File:480220 Shichikanyama Kofun etc aerial.jpg|thumb|right|250px|Aerial view of Shichikanyama Kofun and surrounding kofun (February 1948). North is at the top. <small>{{National Air Photograph}}</small>]] The kofun was located just northwest of the {{ill|Kamizuka Misanzai Kofun|ja|上石津ミサンザイ古墳|1=Kami-Ishizu Misanzai Kofun}} (attributed to {{ill|Emperor Richū|ja|履中天皇}}), near its northern moat. It was considered a {{ill|satellite tomb|ja|陪塚}} of the Shichikannon Kofun. The mound measured 56 meters in diameter and 8 meters in height, featuring moats, stone paving, and haniwa, including cylindrical haniwa and house-shaped haniwa. burial goods from the mound suggest it was constructed in two tiers, with a clay burial chamber detected in three locations.
== Excavation and Findings ==
burial goods recovered during excavations in 1913, 1947, and prior to its destruction in 1952 included: * '''Armor and weapons''': Seven horned helmets (衝角付冑), six or more short-armored cuirasses (短甲), approximately 130 iron swords, serpentine iron swords (蛇行状鉄剣), iron spears, and over 100 iron arrowheads. * '''Horse gear''': Ancient stirrups, regarded as the oldest found in Japan, and gilt bronze fittings for belts, likely imported or influenced by technology from the Korean Peninsula. * '''Other items''': Magatama made from talc, iron axes, chisels, and ceremonial objects.
The bronze belt fittings featured exquisite craftsmanship, including openwork dragon motifs, which closely resemble items found in the {{ill|Imdang-dong Tomb No. 7B|ja|林堂洞7B号墳}} in Gyeongsangbuk-do, Korea. This suggests a connection between the craftsmen or shared cultural influences.
== Historical Significance ==
Despite the extensive grave goods, no human remains were identified in the tomb. This indicates the possibility that it served as a ceremonial site rather than a Kofun, providing valuable insight into the nature of {{ill|satellite tomb|lt=satellite tombs|ja|陪塚}} in the kofun period. Today, the site lies within the grounds of {{ill|Daisen Park|ja|大仙公園}}, where a reconstructed mound of similar size commemorates the original kofun.

The mound measured 56 meters in diameter and 8 meters in height, featuring moats, stone paving, and haniwa, including cylindrical haniwa and house-shaped haniwa. burial goods from the mound suggest it was constructed in two tiers, with a clay burial chamber detected in three locations.
== Excavation and Findings ==
burial goods recovered during excavations in 1913, 1947, and prior to its destruction in 1952 included: * '''Armor and weapons''': Seven horned helmets (衝角付冑), six or more short-armored cuirasses (短甲), approximately 130 iron swords, serpentine iron swords (蛇行状鉄剣), iron spears, and over 100 iron arrowheads. * '''Horse gear''': Ancient stirrups, regarded as the oldest found in Japan, and gilt bronze fittings for belts, likely imported or influenced by technology from the Korean Peninsula. * '''Other items''': Magatama made from talc, iron axes, chisels, and ceremonial objects.
The bronze belt fittings featured exquisite craftsmanship, including openwork dragon motifs, which closely resemble items found in the {{ill|Imdang-dong Tomb No. 7B|ja|林堂洞7B号墳}} in Gyeongsangbuk-do, Korea. This suggests a connection between the craftsmen or shared cultural influences.
== Historical Significance ==
Despite the extensive grave goods, no human remains were identified in the tomb. This indicates the possibility that it served as a ceremonial site rather than a Kofun, providing valuable insight into the nature of {{ill|satellite tomb|lt=satellite tombs|ja|陪塚}} in the kofun period. Today, the site lies within the grounds of {{ill|Daisen Park|ja|大仙公園}}, where a reconstructed mound of similar size commemorates the original kofun.
== References == {{Reflist}}
== Further Reading == * {{Cite book|ja|author=Sakai City Cultural Affairs Department|title=Heritage of Sakai: The Mozu Kofun Group|publisher=Sakai City|year=2008|pages=15|ref=harv}} * {{Cite book|ja|last=Takada|first=Kanta|chapter=The Significance of Kofun-period Ornaments|title=Seasonal Archaeology No. 106|publisher=Yuzankaku|year=2009|pages=57-61|ref=harv}} * {{Cite book|ja|last=Iida|first=Hiromitsu|chapter=Belt Fittings of Shichikanyama Kofun|title=Autumn Special Exhibit Catalogue: The Tombs of Mozu and Furuichi|publisher=Chikatsu Asuka Museum|year=2011|pages=110-111|ref=harv}} * {{Cite book|ja|author=Sakai City Cultural Affairs Bureau|title=Black Weapons, Silver Armor: The Mozu Kofun Group and 5th-Century Conflicts|series=Sakai City Historic Site Lecture Series Vol. 6|year=2013|ref=harv}} * {{Cite book|ja|author=Shichikan Kofun Research Group|title=Research on Shichikanyama Kofun: Reexamination of the burial goods Recovered in 1947 and 1952|publisher=Shinyosha|year=2014|ref=harv}}
== Related Articles == * {{ill|Mozu Kofun Group|ja|百舌鳥古墳群|1=Mozu Tombs}} * {{ill|Nochi Kofun|ja|野中古墳}}
== External Links == * [https://www.city.sakai.lg.jp/kanko/rekishi/dkofun/database/shichikanyama.html Digital Kofun Encyclopedia: Shichikanyama Kofun] (Sakai City Official Website)

'''Shichikanyama Kofun''' (七観山古墳), also known as '''Shichikan Kofun''' (七観古墳), was a {{ill|Round Kofun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|12=draft}} located in Asahigaoka Nakamachi 4-chome, {{ill|Sakai-ku|ja|堺区|12=draft}}, {{ill|Sakai|ja|堺市|12=draft}}, Osaka Prefecture. Part of the {{ill|Mozu Kofun Group|ja|百舌鳥古墳群|1=Mozu Tombs|12=draft}}, the mound was excavated during the Taishō period and again after World War II, yielding a large quantity of iron weapons and armor. It was destroyed in 1952 during land development.
== Overview ==
[[File:480220 Shichikanyama Kofun etc aerial.jpg|thumb|right|250px|Aerial view of Shichikanyama Kofun and surrounding kofun (February 1948). North is at the top. <small>{{National Air Photograph}}</small>]] The kofun was located just northwest of the {{ill|Kamizuka Misanzai Kofun|ja|上石津ミサンザイ古墳|1=Kami-Ishizu Misanzai Kofun|12=draft}} (attributed to {{ill|Emperor Richū|ja|履中天皇|12=draft}}), near its northern moat. It was considered a {{ill|satellite tomb|ja|陪塚|12=draft}} of the Shichikannon Kofun. The mound measured 56 meters in diameter and 8 meters in height, featuring moats, stone paving, and haniwa, including cylindrical haniwa and house-shaped haniwa. burial goods from the mound suggest it was constructed in two tiers, with a clay burial chamber detected in three locations.
== Excavation and Findings ==
burial goods recovered during excavations in 1913, 1947, and prior to its destruction in 1952 included: * '''Armor and weapons''': Seven horned helmets (衝角付冑), six or more short-armored cuirasses (短甲), approximately 130 iron swords, serpentine iron swords (蛇行状鉄剣), iron spears, and over 100 iron arrowheads. * '''Horse gear''': Ancient stirrups, regarded as the oldest found in Japan, and gilt bronze fittings for belts, likely imported or influenced by technology from the Korean Peninsula. * '''Other items''': Magatama made from talc, iron axes, chisels, and ceremonial objects.
The bronze belt fittings featured exquisite craftsmanship, including openwork dragon motifs, which closely resemble items found in the {{ill|Imdang-dong Tomb No. 7B|ja|林堂洞7B号墳|12=draft}} in Gyeongsangbuk-do, Korea. This suggests a connection between the craftsmen or shared cultural influences.
== Historical Significance ==
Despite the extensive grave goods, no human remains were identified in the tomb. This indicates the possibility that it served as a ceremonial site rather than a Kofun, providing valuable insight into the nature of {{ill|satellite tomb|lt=satellite tombs|ja|陪塚|12=draft}} in the kofun period. Today, the site lies within the grounds of {{ill|Daisen Park|ja|大仙公園|12=draft}}, where a reconstructed mound of similar size commemorates the original kofun.
== References == {{Reflist}}
== Further Reading == * {{Cite book|ja|author=Sakai City Cultural Affairs Department|title=Heritage of Sakai: The Mozu Kofun Group|publisher=Sakai City|year=2008|pages=15|ref=harv}} * {{Cite book|ja|last=Takada|first=Kanta|chapter=The Significance of Kofun-period Ornaments|title=Seasonal Archaeology No. 106|publisher=Yuzankaku|year=2009|pages=57-61|ref=harv}} * {{Cite book|ja|last=Iida|first=Hiromitsu|chapter=Belt Fittings of Shichikanyama Kofun|title=Autumn Special Exhibit Catalogue: The Tombs of Mozu and Furuichi|publisher=Chikatsu Asuka Museum|year=2011|pages=110-111|ref=harv}} * {{Cite book|ja|author=Sakai City Cultural Affairs Bureau|title=Black Weapons, Silver Armor: The Mozu Kofun Group and 5th-Century Conflicts|series=Sakai City Historic Site Lecture Series Vol. 6|year=2013|ref=harv}} * {{Cite book|ja|author=Shichikan Kofun Research Group|title=Research on Shichikanyama Kofun: Reexamination of the burial goods Recovered in 1947 and 1952|publisher=Shinyosha|year=2014|ref=harv}}
== Related Articles == * {{ill|Mozu Kofun Group|ja|百舌鳥古墳群|1=Mozu Tombs|12=draft}} * {{ill|Nochi Kofun|ja|野中古墳|12=draft}}
== External Links == * [https://www.city.sakai.lg.jp/kanko/rekishi/dkofun/database/shichikanyama.html Digital Kofun Encyclopedia: Shichikanyama Kofun] (Sakai City Official Website)

'''Shichikanyama Kofun''' (七観山古墳), also known as '''Shichikan Kofun''' (七観古墳), was a {{ill|Round Kofun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|qq=|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} located in Asahigaoka Nakamachi 4-chome, [[:en:Sakai-ku, Sakai|Sakai-ku]], [[:en:Sakai|Sakai]], Osaka Prefecture. Part of the [[:en:Mozu Tombs|Mozu Tombs]], the mound was excavated during the Taishō period and again after World War II, yielding a large quantity of iron weapons and armor. It was destroyed in 1952 during land development.

== Overview ==

[[File:480220 Shichikanyama Kofun etc aerial.jpg|thumb|right|250px|Aerial view of Shichikanyama Kofun and surrounding kofun (February 1948). North is at the top. <small>{{National Air Photograph}}</small>]]
The kofun was located just northwest of the {{ill|Kamizuka Misanzai Kofun|ja|上石津ミサンザイ古墳|1=Kami-Ishizu Misanzai Kofun|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamizuka Misanzai Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Kami-Ishizu Misanzai Kofun|qq=}} (attributed to [[:en:Emperor Richū|Emperor Richū]]), near its northern moat. It was considered a {{ill|satellite tomb|ja|陪塚|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/satellite tomb|qq=|1=Baizuka|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11658220}} of the Shichikannon Kofun. The mound measured 56 meters in diameter and 8 meters in height, featuring moats, stone paving, and haniwa, including cylindrical haniwa and house-shaped haniwa. burial goods from the mound suggest it was constructed in two tiers, with a clay burial chamber detected in three locations.

== Excavation and Findings ==

burial goods recovered during excavations in 1913, 1947, and prior to its destruction in 1952 included:
* '''Armor and weapons''': Seven horned helmets (衝角付冑), six or more short-armored cuirasses (短甲), approximately 130 iron swords, serpentine iron swords (蛇行状鉄剣), iron spears, and over 100 iron arrowheads.
* '''Horse gear''': Ancient stirrups, regarded as the oldest found in Japan, and gilt bronze fittings for belts, likely imported or influenced by technology from the Korean Peninsula.
* '''Other items''': Magatama made from talc, iron axes, chisels, and ceremonial objects.

The bronze belt fittings featured exquisite craftsmanship, including openwork dragon motifs, which closely resemble items found in the {{ill|Imdang-dong Tomb No. 7B|ja|林堂洞7B号墳|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Imdang-dong Tomb No. 7B|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} in Gyeongsangbuk-do, Korea. This suggests a connection between the craftsmen or shared cultural influences.

== Historical Significance ==

Despite the extensive grave goods, no human remains were identified in the tomb. This indicates the possibility that it served as a ceremonial site rather than a Kofun, providing valuable insight into the nature of {{ill|satellite tomb|lt=satellite tombs|ja|陪塚|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/satellite tomb|qq=|1=Baizuka|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11658220}} in the kofun period. Today, the site lies within the grounds of {{ill|Daisen Park|ja|大仙公園|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Daisen Park|qq=}}, where a reconstructed mound of similar size commemorates the original kofun.

== References ==
{{Reflist}}

== Further Reading ==
* {{Cite book|ja|author=Sakai City Cultural Affairs Department|title=Heritage of Sakai: The Mozu Kofun Group|publisher=Sakai City|year=2008|pages=15|ref=harv}}
* {{Cite book|ja|last=Takada|first=Kanta|chapter=The Significance of Kofun-period Ornaments|title=Seasonal Archaeology No. 106|publisher=Yuzankaku|year=2009|pages=57-61|ref=harv}}
* {{Cite book|ja|last=Iida|first=Hiromitsu|chapter=Belt Fittings of Shichikanyama Kofun|title=Autumn Special Exhibit Catalogue: The Tombs of Mozu and Furuichi|publisher=Chikatsu Asuka Museum|year=2011|pages=110-111|ref=harv}}
* {{Cite book|ja|author=Sakai City Cultural Affairs Bureau|title=Black Weapons, Silver Armor: The Mozu Kofun Group and 5th-Century Conflicts|series=Sakai City Historic Site Lecture Series Vol. 6|year=2013|ref=harv}}
* {{Cite book|ja|author=Shichikan Kofun Research Group|title=Research on Shichikanyama Kofun: Reexamination of the burial goods Recovered in 1947 and 1952|publisher=Shinyosha|year=2014|ref=harv}}

== Related Articles ==

'''Shichikanyama Kofun''' (七観山古墳), also known as '''Shichikan Kofun''' (七観古墳), was a {{ill|Round Kofun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|qq=|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} located in Asahigaoka Nakamachi 4-chome, [[:en:Sakai-ku, Sakai|Sakai-ku]], [[:en:Sakai|Sakai]], Osaka Prefecture. Part of the [[:en:Mozu Tombs|Mozu Tombs]], the mound was excavated during the Taishō period and again after World War II, yielding a large quantity of iron weapons and armor. It was destroyed in 1952 during land development.

== Overview ==

[[File:480220 Shichikanyama Kofun etc aerial.jpg|thumb|right|250px|Aerial view of Shichikanyama Kofun and surrounding kofun (February 1948). North is at the top. <small>{{National Air Photograph}}</small>]]
The kofun was located just northwest of the {{ill|Kamizuka Misanzai Kofun|ja|上石津ミサンザイ古墳|1=Kami-Ishizu Misanzai Kofun|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamizuka Misanzai Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Kami-Ishizu Misanzai Kofun|qq=}} (attributed to [[:en:Emperor Richū|Emperor Richū]]), near its northern moat. It was considered a {{ill|satellite tomb|ja|陪塚|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/satellite tomb|qq=|1=Baizuka|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11658220}} of the Shichikannon Kofun. The mound measured 56 meters in diameter and 8 meters in height, featuring moats, stone paving, and haniwa, including cylindrical haniwa and house-shaped haniwa. burial goods from the mound suggest it was constructed in two tiers, with a clay burial chamber detected in three locations.

== Excavation and Findings ==

burial goods recovered during excavations in 1913, 1947, and prior to its destruction in 1952 included:
* '''Armor and weapons''': Seven horned helmets (衝角付冑), six or more short-armored cuirasses (短甲), approximately 130 iron swords, serpentine iron swords (蛇行状鉄剣), iron spears, and over 100 iron arrowheads.
* '''Horse gear''': Ancient stirrups, regarded as the oldest found in Japan, and gilt bronze fittings for belts, likely imported or influenced by technology from the Korean Peninsula.
* '''Other items''': Magatama made from talc, iron axes, chisels, and ceremonial objects.

The bronze belt fittings featured exquisite craftsmanship, including openwork dragon motifs, which closely resemble items found in the {{ill|Imdang-dong Tomb No. 7B|ja|林堂洞7B号墳|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Imdang-dong Tomb No. 7B|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} in Gyeongsangbuk-do, Korea. This suggests a connection between the craftsmen or shared cultural influences.

== Historical Significance ==

Despite the extensive grave goods, no human remains were identified in the tomb. This indicates the possibility that it served as a ceremonial site rather than a Kofun, providing valuable insight into the nature of {{ill|satellite tomb|lt=satellite tombs|ja|陪塚|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/satellite tomb|qq=|1=Baizuka|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11658220}} in the kofun period. Today, the site lies within the grounds of {{ill|Daisen Park|ja|大仙公園|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Daisen Park|qq=}}, where a reconstructed mound of similar size commemorates the original kofun.

* {{Cite book|ja|last=Takada|first=Kanta|chapter=The Significance of Kofun-period Ornaments|title=Seasonal Archaeology No. 106|publisher=Yuzankaku|year=2009|pages=57-61|ref=harv}}
* {{Cite book|ja|last=Iida|first=Hiromitsu|chapter=Belt Fittings of Shichikanyama Kofun|title=Autumn Special Exhibit Catalogue: The Tombs of Mozu and Furuichi|publisher=Chikatsu Asuka Museum|year=2011|pages=110-111|ref=harv}}
* {{Cite book|ja|author=Sakai City Cultural Affairs Bureau|title=Black Weapons, Silver Armor: The Mozu Kofun Group and 5th-Century Conflicts|series=Sakai City Historic Site Lecture Series Vol. 6|year=2013|ref=harv}}
* {{Cite book|ja|author=Shichikan Kofun Research Group|title=Research on Shichikanyama Kofun: Reexamination of the burial goods Recovered in 1947 and 1952|publisher=Shinyosha|year=2014|ref=harv}}

'''Shichikanyama Kofun''' (七観山古墳), also known as '''Shichikan Kofun''' (七観古墳), was a {{ill|Round Kofun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} located in Asahigaoka Nakamachi 4-chome, [[:en:Sakai-ku, Sakai|Sakai-ku]], [[:en:Sakai|Sakai]], Osaka Prefecture. Part of the [[:en:Mozu Tombs|Mozu Tombs]], the mound was excavated during the Taishō period and again after World War II, yielding a large quantity of iron weapons and armor. It was destroyed in 1952 during land development.

== Overview ==

[[File:480220 Shichikanyama Kofun etc aerial.jpg|thumb|right|250px|Aerial view of Shichikanyama Kofun and surrounding kofun (February 1948). North is at the top. <small>{{National Air Photograph}}</small>]]
The kofun was located just northwest of the {{ill|Kamizuka Misanzai Kofun|ja|上石津ミサンザイ古墳|1=Kami-Ishizu Misanzai Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamizuka Misanzai Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Kami-Ishizu Misanzai Kofun|qq=}} (attributed to [[:en:Emperor Richū|Emperor Richū]]), near its northern moat. It was considered a {{ill|satellite tomb|ja|陪塚|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/satellite tomb|1=Baizuka|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11658220}} of the Shichikannon Kofun. The mound measured 56 meters in diameter and 8 meters in height, featuring moats, stone paving, and haniwa, including cylindrical haniwa and house-shaped haniwa. burial goods from the mound suggest it was constructed in two tiers, with a clay burial chamber detected in three locations.

== Excavation and Findings ==

burial goods recovered during excavations in 1913, 1947, and prior to its destruction in 1952 included:
* '''Armor and weapons''': Seven horned helmets (衝角付冑), six or more short-armored cuirasses (短甲), approximately 130 iron swords, serpentine iron swords (蛇行状鉄剣), iron spears, and over 100 iron arrowheads.
* '''Horse gear''': Ancient stirrups, regarded as the oldest found in Japan, and gilt bronze fittings for belts, likely imported or influenced by technology from the Korean Peninsula.
* '''Other items''': Magatama made from talc, iron axes, chisels, and ceremonial objects.

The bronze belt fittings featured exquisite craftsmanship, including openwork dragon motifs, which closely resemble items found in the {{ill|Imdang-dong Tomb No. 7B|ja|林堂洞7B号墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Imdang-dong Tomb No. 7B|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} in Gyeongsangbuk-do, Korea. This suggests a connection between the craftsmen or shared cultural influences.

== Historical Significance ==

Despite the extensive grave goods, no human remains were identified in the tomb. This indicates the possibility that it served as a ceremonial site rather than a Kofun, providing valuable insight into the nature of {{ill|satellite tomb|lt=satellite tombs|ja|陪塚|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/satellite tomb|1=Baizuka|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11658220}} in the kofun period. Today, the site lies within the grounds of {{ill|Daisen Park|ja|大仙公園|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Daisen Park|qq=}}, where a reconstructed mound of similar size commemorates the original kofun.

* {{Cite book|ja|author=Sakai City Cultural Affairs Bureau|title=Black Weapons, Silver Armor: The Mozu Kofun Group and 5th-Century Conflicts|series=Sakai City Historic Site Lecture Series Vol. 6|year=2013|ref=harv}}

'''Shichikanyama Kofun''' (七観山古墳), also known as '''Shichikan Kofun''' (七観古墳), was a {{ill|Round Kofun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} located in Asahigaoka Nakamachi 4-chome, [[:en:Sakai-ku, Sakai|Sakai-ku]], [[:en:Sakai|Sakai]], Osaka Prefecture. Part of the [[:en:Mozu Tombs|Mozu Tombs]], the mound was excavated during the Taishō period and again after World War II, yielding a large quantity of iron weapons and armor. It was destroyed in 1952 during land development.

== Overview ==

[[File:480220 Shichikanyama Kofun etc aerial.jpg|thumb|right|250px|Aerial view of Shichikanyama Kofun and surrounding kofun (February 1948). North is at the top. <small>{{National Air Photograph}}</small>]]
The kofun was located just northwest of the {{ill|Kamizuka Misanzai Kofun|ja|上石津ミサンザイ古墳|1=Kami-Ishizu Misanzai Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamizuka Misanzai Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Kami-Ishizu Misanzai Kofun|qq=}} (attributed to [[:en:Emperor Richū|Emperor Richū]]), near its northern moat. It was considered a {{ill|satellite tomb|ja|陪塚|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/satellite tomb|1=Baizuka|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11658220}} of the Shichikannon Kofun. The mound measured 56 meters in diameter and 8 meters in height, featuring moats, stone paving, and haniwa, including cylindrical haniwa and house-shaped haniwa. burial goods from the mound suggest it was constructed in two tiers, with a clay burial chamber detected in three locations.

== Excavation and Findings ==

burial goods recovered during excavations in 1913, 1947, and prior to its destruction in 1952 included:
* '''Armor and weapons''': Seven horned helmets (衝角付冑), six or more short-armored cuirasses (短甲), approximately 130 iron swords, serpentine iron swords (蛇行状鉄剣), iron spears, and over 100 iron arrowheads.
* '''Horse gear''': Ancient stirrups, regarded as the oldest found in Japan, and gilt bronze fittings for belts, likely imported or influenced by technology from the Korean Peninsula.
* '''Other items''': Magatama made from talc, iron axes, chisels, and ceremonial objects.

The bronze belt fittings featured exquisite craftsmanship, including openwork dragon motifs, which closely resemble items found in the {{ill|Imdang-dong Tomb No. 7B|ja|林堂洞7B号墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Imdang-dong Tomb No. 7B|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} in Gyeongsangbuk-do, Korea. This suggests a connection between the craftsmen or shared cultural influences.

== Historical Significance ==

Despite the extensive grave goods, no human remains were identified in the tomb. This indicates the possibility that it served as a ceremonial site rather than a Kofun, providing valuable insight into the nature of {{ill|satellite tomb|lt=satellite tombs|ja|陪塚|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/satellite tomb|1=Baizuka|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11658220}} in the kofun period. Today, the site lies within the grounds of {{ill|Daisen Park|ja|大仙公園|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Daisen Park|qq=}}, where a reconstructed mound of similar size commemorates the original kofun.

The bronze belt fittings featured exquisite craftsmanship, including openwork dragon motifs, which closely resemble items found in the {{ill|Imdang-dong Tomb No. 7B|ja|林堂洞7B号墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Imdang-dong Tomb No. 7B|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} in Gyeongsangbuk-do, Korea. This suggests a connection between the craftsmen or shared cultural influences.

== Historical Significance ==

Despite the extensive grave goods, no human remains were identified in the tomb. This indicates the possibility that it served as a ceremonial site rather than a Kofun, providing valuable insight into the nature of {{ill|satellite tomb|lt=satellite tombs|ja|陪塚|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/satellite tomb|1=Baizuka|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11658220}} in the kofun period. Today, the site lies within the grounds of {{ill|Daisen Park|ja|大仙公園|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Daisen Park|qq=}}, where a reconstructed mound of similar size commemorates the original kofun.

'''Shichikanyama Kofun''' (七観山古墳), also known as '''Shichikan Kofun''' (七観古墳), was a {{ill|Round Kofun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} located in Asahigaoka Nakamachi 4-chome, [[:en:Sakai-ku, Sakai|Sakai-ku]], [[:en:Sakai|Sakai]], Osaka Prefecture. Part of the [[:en:Mozu Tombs|Mozu Tombs]], the mound was excavated during the Taishō period and again after World War II, yielding a large quantity of iron weapons and armor. It was destroyed in 1952 during land development.

== Overview ==

[[File:480220 Shichikanyama Kofun etc aerial.jpg|thumb|right|250px|Aerial view of Shichikanyama Kofun and surrounding kofun (February 1948). North is at the top. <small>{{National Air Photograph}}</small>]]
The kofun was located just northwest of the {{ill|Kamizuka Misanzai Kofun|ja|上石津ミサンザイ古墳|1=Kami-Ishizu Misanzai Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamizuka Misanzai Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Kami-Ishizu Misanzai Kofun|qq=}} (attributed to [[:en:Emperor Richū|Emperor Richū]]), near its northern moat. It was considered a {{ill|satellite tomb|ja|陪塚|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/satellite tomb|1=Baizuka|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11658220}} of the Shichikannon Kofun. The mound measured 56 meters in diameter and 8 meters in height, featuring moats, stone paving, and haniwa, including cylindrical haniwa and house-shaped haniwa. burial goods from the mound suggest it was constructed in two tiers, with a clay burial chamber detected in three locations.

== Excavation and Findings ==

burial goods recovered during excavations in 1913, 1947, and prior to its destruction in 1952 included:
* '''Armor and weapons''': Seven horned helmets (衝角付冑), six or more short-armored cuirasses (短甲), approximately 130 iron swords, serpentine iron swords (蛇行状鉄剣), iron spears, and over 100 iron arrowheads.
* '''Horse gear''': Ancient stirrups, regarded as the oldest found in Japan, and gilt bronze fittings for belts, likely imported or influenced by technology from the Korean Peninsula.
* '''Other items''': Magatama made from talc, iron axes, chisels, and ceremonial objects.

The bronze belt fittings featured exquisite craftsmanship, including openwork dragon motifs, which closely resemble items found in the {{ill|Imdang-dong Tomb No. 7B|ja|林堂洞7B号墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Imdang-dong Tomb No. 7B|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} in Gyeongsangbuk-do, Korea. This suggests a connection between the craftsmen or shared cultural influences.

== Historical Significance ==

Despite the extensive grave goods, no human remains were identified in the tomb. This indicates the possibility that it served as a ceremonial site rather than a Kofun, providing valuable insight into the nature of {{ill|satellite tomb|lt=satellite tombs|ja|陪塚|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/satellite tomb|1=Baizuka|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11658220}} in the kofun period. Today, the site lies within the grounds of {{ill|Daisen Park|ja|大仙公園|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Daisen Park|qq=}}, where a reconstructed mound of similar size commemorates the original kofun.

It was destroyed in 1952 during land development.

[[File:480220 Shichikanyama Kofun etc aerial.jpg|thumb|right|250px|Aerial view of Shichikanyama Kofun and surrounding kofun (February 1948). North is at the top. <small>{{National Air Photograph}}</small>]]
The kofun was located just northwest of the {{ill|Kamizuka Misanzai Kofun|ja|上石津ミサンザイ古墳|1=Kami-Ishizu Misanzai Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamizuka Misanzai Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Kami-Ishizu Misanzai Kofun|qq=}} (attributed to [[:en:Emperor Richū|Emperor Richū]]), near its northern moat. It was considered a {{ill|satellite tomb|ja|陪塚|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/satellite tomb|1=Baizuka|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11658220}} of the Shichikannon Kofun. The mound measured 56 meters in diameter and 8 meters in height, featuring moats, stone paving, and haniwa, including cylindrical haniwa and house-shaped haniwa. burial goods from the mound suggest it was constructed in two tiers, with a clay burial chamber detected in three locations.

== Excavation and Findings ==

burial goods recovered during excavations in 1913, 1947, and prior to its destruction in 1952 included:
* '''Armor and weapons''': Seven horned helmets (衝角付冑), six or more short-armored cuirasses (短甲), approximately 130 iron swords, serpentine iron swords (蛇行状鉄剣), iron spears, and over 100 iron arrowheads.
* '''Horse gear''': Ancient stirrups, regarded as the oldest found in Japan, and gilt bronze fittings for belts, likely imported or influenced by technology from the Korean Peninsula.
* '''Other items''': Magatama made from talc, iron axes, chisels, and ceremonial objects.

The bronze belt fittings featured exquisite craftsmanship, including openwork dragon motifs, which closely resemble items found in the {{ill|Imdang-dong Tomb No. 7B|ja|林堂洞7B号墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Imdang-dong Tomb No. 7B|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} in Gyeongsangbuk-do, Korea. This suggests a connection between the craftsmen or shared cultural influences.

== Historical Significance ==

Despite the extensive grave goods, no human remains were identified in the tomb. This indicates the possibility that it served as a ceremonial site rather than a Kofun, providing valuable insight into the nature of {{ill|satellite tomb|lt=satellite tombs|ja|陪塚|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/satellite tomb|1=Baizuka|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11658220}} in the kofun period. Today, the site lies within the grounds of {{ill|Daisen Park|ja|大仙公園|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Daisen Park|qq=}}, where a reconstructed mound of similar size commemorates the original kofun.

'''Shichikanyama Kofun''' (七観山古墳), also known as '''Shichikan Kofun''' (七観古墳), was a {{ill|Round Kofun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} located in Asahigaoka Nakamachi 4-chome, [[:en:Sakai-ku, Sakai|Sakai-ku]], [[:en:Sakai|Sakai]], Osaka Prefecture. Part of the [[:en:Mozu Tombs|Mozu Tombs]], the mound was excavated during the Taishō period and again after World War II, yielding a large quantity of iron weapons and armor. It was destroyed in 1952 during land development.

== Overview ==

[[File:480220 Shichikanyama Kofun etc aerial.jpg|thumb|right|250px|Aerial view of Shichikanyama Kofun and surrounding kofun (February 1948). North is at the top. <small>{{National Air Photograph}}</small>]]
The kofun was located just northwest of the {{ill|Kamizuka Misanzai Kofun|ja|上石津ミサンザイ古墳|1=Kami-Ishizu Misanzai Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamizuka Misanzai Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Kami-Ishizu Misanzai Kofun|qq=}} (attributed to [[:en:Emperor Richū|Emperor Richū]]), near its northern moat. It was considered a {{ill|satellite tomb|ja|陪塚|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/satellite tomb|1=Baizuka|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11658220}} of the Shichikannon Kofun. The mound measured 56 meters in diameter and 8 meters in height, featuring moats, stone paving, and haniwa, including cylindrical haniwa and house-shaped haniwa. burial goods from the mound suggest it was constructed in two tiers, with a clay burial chamber detected in three locations.

== Excavation and Findings ==

burial goods recovered during excavations in 1913, 1947, and prior to its destruction in 1952 included:
* '''Armor and weapons''': Seven horned helmets (衝角付冑), six or more short-armored cuirasses (短甲), approximately 130 iron swords, serpentine iron swords (蛇行状鉄剣), iron spears, and over 100 iron arrowheads.
* '''Horse gear''': Ancient stirrups, regarded as the oldest found in Japan, and gilt bronze fittings for belts, likely imported or influenced by technology from the Korean Peninsula.
* '''Other items''': Magatama made from talc, iron axes, chisels, and ceremonial objects.

The bronze belt fittings featured exquisite craftsmanship, including openwork dragon motifs, which closely resemble items found in the {{ill|Imdang-dong Tomb No. 7B|ja|林堂洞7B号墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Imdang-dong Tomb No. 7B|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} in Gyeongsangbuk-do, Korea. This suggests a connection between the craftsmen or shared cultural influences.

== Historical Significance ==

Despite the extensive grave goods, no human remains were identified in the tomb. This indicates the possibility that it served as a ceremonial site rather than a Kofun, providing valuable insight into the nature of {{ill|satellite tomb|lt=satellite tombs|ja|陪塚|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/satellite tomb|1=Baizuka|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11658220}} in the kofun period. Today, the site lies within the grounds of {{ill|Daisen Park|ja|大仙公園|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Daisen Park|qq=}}, where a reconstructed mound of similar size commemorates the original kofun.

* {{Cite book|ja|author=Sakai City Cultural Affairs Bureau|title=Black Weapons, Silver Armor: The Mozu Kofun Group and 5th-Century Conflicts|series=Sakai City Historic Site Lecture Series Vol. 6|year=2013|ref=harv}}
* {{Cite book|ja|author=Shichikan Kofun Research Group|title=Research on Shichikanyama Kofun: Reexamination of the burial goods Recovered in 1947 and 1952|publisher=Shinyosha|year=2014|ref=harv}}

'''Shichikanyama Kofun''' (七観山古墳), also known as '''Shichikan Kofun''' (七観古墳), was a {{ill|Round Kofun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} located in Asahigaoka Nakamachi 4-chome, {{ill|Sakai-ku, Sakai|en|Sakai-ku, Sakai|WD=Q1042008|lt=Sakai-ku}}, {{ill|Sakai|en|Sakai|WD=Q193428|lt=Sakai}}, Osaka Prefecture. Part of the {{ill|Mozu Tombs|en|Mozu Tombs|WD=Q4250584|lt=Mozu Tombs}}, the mound was excavated during the Taishō period and again after World War II, yielding a large quantity of iron weapons and armor. It was destroyed in 1952 during land development.

== Overview ==

[[File:480220 Shichikanyama Kofun etc aerial.jpg|thumb|right|250px|Aerial view of Shichikanyama Kofun and surrounding kofun (February 1948). North is at the top. <small>{{National Air Photograph}}</small>]]
The kofun was located just northwest of the {{ill|Kamizuka Misanzai Kofun|ja|上石津ミサンザイ古墳|1=Kami-Ishizu Misanzai Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamizuka Misanzai Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Kami-Ishizu Misanzai Kofun|qq=}} (attributed to {{ill|Emperor Richū|en|Emperor Richū|WD=Q329704|lt=Emperor Richū}}), near its northern moat. It was considered a {{ill|satellite tomb|ja|陪塚|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/satellite tomb|1=Baizuka|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11658220}} of the Shichikannon Kofun. The mound measured 56 meters in diameter and 8 meters in height, featuring moats, stone paving, and haniwa, including cylindrical haniwa and house-shaped haniwa. burial goods from the mound suggest it was constructed in two tiers, with a clay burial chamber detected in three locations.

== Excavation and Findings ==

burial goods recovered during excavations in 1913, 1947, and prior to its destruction in 1952 included:
* '''Armor and weapons''': Seven horned helmets (衝角付冑), six or more short-armored cuirasses (短甲), approximately 130 iron swords, serpentine iron swords (蛇行状鉄剣), iron spears, and over 100 iron arrowheads.
* '''Horse gear''': Ancient stirrups, regarded as the oldest found in Japan, and gilt bronze fittings for belts, likely imported or influenced by technology from the Korean Peninsula.
* '''Other items''': Magatama made from talc, iron axes, chisels, and ceremonial objects.

The bronze belt fittings featured exquisite craftsmanship, including openwork dragon motifs, which closely resemble items found in the {{ill|Imdang-dong Tomb No. 7B|ja|林堂洞7B号墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Imdang-dong Tomb No. 7B|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} in Gyeongsangbuk-do, Korea. This suggests a connection between the craftsmen or shared cultural influences.

== Historical Significance ==

Despite the extensive grave goods, no human remains were identified in the tomb. This indicates the possibility that it served as a ceremonial site rather than a Kofun, providing valuable insight into the nature of {{ill|satellite tomb|lt=satellite tombs|ja|陪塚|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/satellite tomb|1=Baizuka|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11658220}} in the kofun period. Today, the site lies within the grounds of {{ill|Daisen Park|ja|大仙公園|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Daisen Park|qq=}}, where a reconstructed mound of similar size commemorates the original kofun.

* {{Cite book|ja|last=Takada|first=Kanta|chapter=The Significance of Kofun-period Ornaments|title=Seasonal Archaeology No. 106|publisher=Yuzankaku|year=2009|pages=57-61|ref=harv}}
* {{Cite book|ja|last=Iida|first=Hiromitsu|chapter=Belt Fittings of Shichikanyama Kofun|title=Autumn Special Exhibit Catalogue: The Tombs of Mozu and Furuichi|publisher=Chikatsu Asuka Museum|year=2011|pages=110-111|ref=harv}}
* {{Cite book|ja|author=Sakai City Cultural Affairs Bureau|title=Black Weapons, Silver Armor: The Mozu Kofun Group and 5th-Century Conflicts|series=Sakai City Historic Site Lecture Series Vol. 6|year=2013|ref=harv}}
* {{Cite book|ja|author=Shichikan Kofun Research Group|title=Research on Shichikanyama Kofun: Reexamination of the burial goods Recovered in 1947 and 1952|publisher=Shinyosha|year=2014|ref=harv}}

|architectural_styles= {{ill|circular kofun|WD=Q11394747}}

'''Shichikanyama Kofun''' (七観山古墳), also known as '''Shichikan Kofun''' (七観古墳), was a {{ill|Round Kofun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} located in Asahigaoka Nakamachi 4-chome, {{ill|Sakai-ku, Sakai|en|Sakai-ku, Sakai|WD=Q1042008|lt=Sakai-ku}}, {{ill|Sakai|en|Sakai|WD=Q193428|lt=Sakai}}, Osaka Prefecture. Part of the {{ill|Mozu Tombs|en|Mozu Tombs|WD=Q4250584|lt=Mozu Tombs}}, the mound was excavated during the Taishō period and again after World War II, yielding a large quantity of iron weapons and armor. It was destroyed in 1952 during land development.

== Overview ==

[[File:480220 Shichikanyama Kofun etc aerial.jpg|thumb|right|250px|Aerial view of Shichikanyama Kofun and surrounding kofun (February 1948). North is at the top. <small>{{National Air Photograph}}</small>]]
The kofun was located just northwest of the {{ill|Kamizuka Misanzai Kofun|ja|上石津ミサンザイ古墳|1=Kami-Ishizu Misanzai Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamizuka Misanzai Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Kami-Ishizu Misanzai Kofun|qq=}} (attributed to {{ill|Emperor Richū|en|Emperor Richū|WD=Q329704|lt=Emperor Richū}}), near its northern moat. It was considered a {{ill|satellite tomb|ja|陪塚|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/satellite tomb|1=Baizuka|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11658220}} of the Shichikannon Kofun. The mound measured 56 meters in diameter and 8 meters in height, featuring moats, stone paving, and haniwa, including cylindrical haniwa and house-shaped haniwa. burial goods from the mound suggest it was constructed in two tiers, with a clay burial chamber detected in three locations.

== Excavation and Findings ==

burial goods recovered during excavations in 1913, 1947, and prior to its destruction in 1952 included:
* '''Armor and weapons''': Seven horned helmets (衝角付冑), six or more short-armored cuirasses (短甲), approximately 130 iron swords, serpentine iron swords (蛇行状鉄剣), iron spears, and over 100 iron arrowheads.
* '''Horse gear''': Ancient stirrups, regarded as the oldest found in Japan, and gilt bronze fittings for belts, likely imported or influenced by technology from the Korean Peninsula.
* '''Other items''': Magatama made from talc, iron axes, chisels, and ceremonial objects.

The bronze belt fittings featured exquisite craftsmanship, including openwork dragon motifs, which closely resemble items found in the {{ill|Imdang-dong Tomb No. 7B|ja|林堂洞7B号墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Imdang-dong Tomb No. 7B|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} in Gyeongsangbuk-do, Korea. This suggests a connection between the craftsmen or shared cultural influences.

== Historical Significance ==

Despite the extensive grave goods, no human remains were identified in the tomb. This indicates the possibility that it served as a ceremonial site rather than a Kofun, providing valuable insight into the nature of {{ill|satellite tomb|lt=satellite tombs|ja|陪塚|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/satellite tomb|1=Baizuka|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11658220}} in the kofun period. Today, the site lies within the grounds of {{ill|Daisen Park|ja|大仙公園|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Daisen Park|qq=}}, where a reconstructed mound of similar size commemorates the original kofun.

== References ==
{{Reflist}}

== Further Reading ==
* {{Cite book|ja|author=Sakai City Cultural Affairs Department|title=Heritage of Sakai: The Mozu Kofun Group|publisher=Sakai City|year=2008|pages=15|ref=harv}}
* {{Cite book|ja|last=Takada|first=Kanta|chapter=The Significance of Kofun-period Ornaments|title=Seasonal Archaeology No. 106|publisher=Yuzankaku|year=2009|pages=57-61|ref=harv}}
* {{Cite book|ja|last=Iida|first=Hiromitsu|chapter=Belt Fittings of Shichikanyama Kofun|title=Autumn Special Exhibit Catalogue: The Tombs of Mozu and Furuichi|publisher=Chikatsu Asuka Museum|year=2011|pages=110-111|ref=harv}}
* {{Cite book|ja|author=Sakai City Cultural Affairs Bureau|title=Black Weapons, Silver Armor: The Mozu Kofun Group and 5th-Century Conflicts|series=Sakai City Historic Site Lecture Series Vol. 6|year=2013|ref=harv}}
* {{Cite book|ja|author=Shichikan Kofun Research Group|title=Research on Shichikanyama Kofun: Reexamination of the burial goods Recovered in 1947 and 1952|publisher=Shinyosha|year=2014|ref=harv}}

== Related Articles ==
* {{ill|Mozu Tombs|en|Mozu Tombs|WD=Q4250584|lt=Mozu Tombs}}
* {{ill|Nonaka Kofun|lt=Nonaka Kofun|lt=Nochi Kofun|ja|野中古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nochi Kofun|qq=}}

== External Links ==

'''Shichikanyama Kofun''' (七観山古墳), also known as '''Shichikan Kofun''' (七観古墳), was a {{ill|Round Kofun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} located in Asahigaoka Nakamachi 4-chome, {{ill|Sakai-ku, Sakai|en|Sakai-ku, Sakai|WD=Q1042008|lt=Sakai-ku|1=Sakai-ku, Sakai}}, {{ill|Sakai|en|Sakai|WD=Q193428|lt=Sakai|1=Sakai}}, Osaka Prefecture. Part of the {{ill|Mozu Tombs|en|Mozu Tombs|WD=Q4250584|lt=Mozu Tombs|1=Mozu Tombs}}, the mound was excavated during the Taishō period and again after World War II, yielding a large quantity of iron weapons and armor. It was destroyed in 1952 during land development.

== Overview ==

[[File:480220 Shichikanyama Kofun etc aerial.jpg|thumb|right|250px|Aerial view of Shichikanyama Kofun and surrounding kofun (February 1948). North is at the top. <small>{{National Air Photograph}}</small>]]
The kofun was located just northwest of the {{ill|Kamizuka Misanzai Kofun|ja|上石津ミサンザイ古墳|1=Kami-Ishizu Misanzai Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamizuka Misanzai Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Kami-Ishizu Misanzai Kofun|qq=}} (attributed to {{ill|Emperor Richū|en|Emperor Richū|WD=Q329704|lt=Emperor Richū|1=Emperor Richū}}), near its northern moat. It was considered a {{ill|satellite tomb|ja|陪塚|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/satellite tomb|1=Baizuka|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11658220}} of the Shichikannon Kofun. The mound measured 56 meters in diameter and 8 meters in height, featuring moats, stone paving, and haniwa, including cylindrical haniwa and house-shaped haniwa. burial goods from the mound suggest it was constructed in two tiers, with a clay burial chamber detected in three locations.

== Excavation and Findings ==

burial goods recovered during excavations in 1913, 1947, and prior to its destruction in 1952 included:
* '''Armor and weapons''': Seven horned helmets (衝角付冑), six or more short-armored cuirasses (短甲), approximately 130 iron swords, serpentine iron swords (蛇行状鉄剣), iron spears, and over 100 iron arrowheads.
* '''Horse gear''': Ancient stirrups, regarded as the oldest found in Japan, and gilt bronze fittings for belts, likely imported or influenced by technology from the Korean Peninsula.
* '''Other items''': Magatama made from talc, iron axes, chisels, and ceremonial objects.

The bronze belt fittings featured exquisite craftsmanship, including openwork dragon motifs, which closely resemble items found in the {{ill|Imdang-dong Tomb No. 7B|ja|林堂洞7B号墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Imdang-dong Tomb No. 7B|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} in Gyeongsangbuk-do, Korea. This suggests a connection between the craftsmen or shared cultural influences.

== Historical Significance ==

Despite the extensive grave goods, no human remains were identified in the tomb. This indicates the possibility that it served as a ceremonial site rather than a Kofun, providing valuable insight into the nature of {{ill|satellite tomb|lt=satellite tombs|ja|陪塚|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/satellite tomb|1=Baizuka|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11658220}} in the kofun period. Today, the site lies within the grounds of {{ill|Daisen Park|ja|大仙公園|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Daisen Park|qq=}}, where a reconstructed mound of similar size commemorates the original kofun.
